Description: In R2013a MATLAB sometimes crashes with glren:XFreePixmap on the stack trace while using print/saveas functionality. Recent versions of MATLAB no longer use this function.

Summary: MATLAB crashes with?uiw:UIW_ColormapIndex2ColorPtr?on the stack trace
Description: Sometimes MATLAB crashes with?UIW_ColormapIndex2ColorPtr ?on the stack trace. The crash is due to a bug in the?UIW_ColormapIndex2ColorPtr ?function that converts indexed colors to RGB colors. Recent versions of MATLAB no longer use this function.?

Summary: Error message when creating or modifying AUTOSAR configuration for AUTOSAR component with long name
Description: If the name of an AUTOSAR software component that you are modeling in Simulink exceeds the defined Maximum SHORT-NAME length , minus four characters, errors might occur when you create or modify the AUTOSAR configuration. Examples include: Assertion failure if you add a receiver port to the component in the AUTOSAR Properties Explorer. Mapping failure if you create a default AUTOSAR component for the model using the AUTOSAR Component Builder.  The AUTOSAR software component name can be modified in the Configure AUTOSAR Interface dialog box, AUTOSAR Properties > AUTOSAR > AtomicComponents  view. The Maximum SHORT-NAME length  is defined in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, AUTOSAR Code Generation Options  pane. The default maximum length is 32.
Workaround: To work around this bug, use one of the following workarounds:Change the Maximum SHORT-NAME length value for the model. For example, the following code conditionally increases the maximum length by four.%%% Workaround: Change AutosarMaxShortNameLength for modeldataObj = autosar.api.getAUTOSARProperties(bdroot);maxShortNameLen = get_param(bdroot, 'AutosarMaxShortNameLength');[~, compName] = fileparts(dataObj.get('XmlOptions', 'ComponentQualifiedName'));if numel(compName) >= maxShortNameLen-4    set_param(bdroot, 'AutosarMaxShortNameLength', maxShortNameLen+4);endShorten the name of the AUTOSAR software component. For example, the follow code truncates the compionent name to length Maximum SHORT-NAME length minus four.%%% Workaround: Shorten name of AUTOSAR ASWCdataObj = autosar.api.getAUTOSARProperties(bdroot);maxShortNameLen = get_param(bdroot, 'AutosarMaxShortNameLength');[compPath, compName] = fileparts(dataObj.get('XmlOptions', 'ComponentQualifiedName'));if numel(compName) >= maxShortNameLen-4    compName = compName(1:end-4);    dataObj.set('XmlOptions', 'ComponentQualifiedName', [compPath '/' compName]);end

Summary: USRP support package installation can error out when installing the dd v0.5 for Windows third-party software
Description: Installing the USRP support package on Windows can trigger an error such as this: Error installing third party software dd v0.5 for Windows: Error executing command "unzip('C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download\dd-0.5.zip', fullfile(sdruroot,'uhdapps','utils'))" Invalid zip file "C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download\dd-0.5.zip".
Workaround: Download the USRP support package to a folder (for example, C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download).In the folder, delete the dd-0.5.zip file. It is not a valid zip file.Use a web browser to download dd-0.5.zip manually from http://www.chrysocome.net/downloads/dd-0.5.zip to C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download.Install the USRP support package from the folder C:\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2014b\downloads\usrpradio_download.

Summary: Deleting a Subsystem block in an open Simulink diagram can crash MATLAB
Description: When you delete a Subsystem block that has Simscape ports from a Simulink diagram (either in a model or library) that is open in a Simulink Editor window, there is a small chance that MATLAB?will crash.
Workaround: To delete that Subsystem block, do not open the system. Instead, load the system and use the command-line API to delete the block. For example:?load_system('sf_car')delete_block('sf_car/transmission')?

Summary: Crash during code generation for certain cases of vector indexing
Description: For certain cases of vector indexing, code generation can cause a crash with a stack trace that terminates in CG::analysis::IndexInfo::mul.  A crash can occur under the following curcumstances: Inside a loop, a vector q  is indexed by a vector n . n  is defined previously, outside the loop. The definition of n  depends on an expression that includes multiplication by another vector a . a  is defined before the definition of n .  For example, in the following code: The vector q  is indexed by n(i ). n  is defined outside the loop that contains the indexing into q . The definition of n  depends on the multiplication of (a+1)  by N . a  is defined before n .  function y = foo(q)N = 1024; M = 512;y = 0;for k = 1:N    j = 1:M;    a = (k-1)/N + (j-1)/M - 1;    n = 1 + N*(a+1);    for i = 1:M        y = y + q(n(i));    endendend ?
Workaround: If you encounter this problem on a regular basis, and need technical assistance regarding the problem, contact MathWorks technical support:https://www.mathworks.com/support/service_requests/contact_support.do?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code for some cases of multiple sort calls with different sort orders in MATLAB Function block
Description: The generated code for sort  in a MATLAB function block can sort in the wrong direction when the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function block calls sort  more than once, with the sorting direction set to 'ascend'  for some calls  and to 'descend'  for other calls. All of the other arguments in the sort  calls have identical values. On the Code Generation>Interface  pane, the Shared code placement  parameter is set to Shared location .   For these conditions, Simulink Coder generates code that sorts in only one order (either ascending or descending).  Depending on certain model properties, this error can also occur when the model's Shared code placement  parameter is set to Auto.  ? ? ?
Workaround: Try configuring the model with Shared code placement parameter set to Auto. This workaround might not succeed for all models.

Summary: Target computer won't boot in R2014a.
Description: Target computer may not boot Simulink Real-Time kernel in R2014a if the following conditions are met: Graphics mode (Target Scope) is enabled The video card in the target computer does not support high-resolution VESA modes. Generally this is the case in older machines  A common visible symptom is a blank screen during boot up.
Workaround: Contact MathWorks technical support to get a R2014a patch that will ensure the target computer successfully boots with a lower resolution display.

Summary: Justifications and comments are not saved automatically when an analysis is relaunched
Description: In the 'Results Manager' perspective of the Polyspace user interface, you have to explicitly save your comments and justifications. Otherwise, when you switch to the 'Project Manager' perspective and relaunch an analysis, you lose your comments and justifications from the previous analysis.If you save using the Ctrl +S keyboard shortcut, make sure that the Polyspace window is in focus. Otherwise, the comments and justifications are not saved.
Workaround: To make sure your comments and justifications are saved, select the menu item 'File>Save' after each review session.

Summary: Attempting to install the Function Wizard on Windows 8.1 may display the error "Compile error in hidden module".
Description: Attempting to install the Function Wizard on Windows 8.1 may display the error "Compile error in hidden module".

Summary: Simulink.sdi.report does not work on Japanese machines
Description: When using the Simulink.sdi.report  function on Japanese machines, an error is displayed indicating the value of 'ReportToCreate'  is invalid.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP file and use the 3 functions in place of Simulink.sdi.report to create the necessary report. Use the same optional arguments as the report function but omit the 'ReportToCreate' option. Example: sdiCreateRunComparisonReport(... 'ColumnsToReport', metaDataOfInterest, ... 'ReportOutputFile', 'sample_report.html', ... 'PreventOverwritingFile', false, ..., 'LaunchReport', true, ... 'SignalsToReport', 'ReportAllSignals');

Summary: Hyperlink indicating warnings missing from editor status bar when model is opened
Description: When you perform a model operation that results in a warning, a hyperlink appears in the status bar of the Simulink Editor. If you click this hyperlink, the Diagnostic Viewer appears, displaying details about the warning. However, Simulink does not display this hyperlink if warnings are generated when you open the model.
Workaround: To inspect warnings generated when you open a model, click View > Diagnostic Viewer in the Simulink Editor.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute time temporal logic operators with expression arguments can produce incorrect results due to improper down casting in the generated code
Description: Using an expression that requires evaluation in an absolute time temporal operator, such as after(DELAY,sec)  or before(10*U,sec) , can cause the expression to sometimes evaluate incorrectly. This will occur when the following conditions are true: The product of the chart's sample time and the granularity of the temporal operator is an integer. The granularity is specified using the following enumerated labels: sec = 1  msec = 1000  usec = 1000000   The result of the expression is greater than the smallest signed integer type that can hold the product.  For example, if the sample time was 0.1, and the units msec , the smallest integer type that can hold the product (100) is int8 . If an absolute time temporal logic operator such as after(DELAY,msec)  was used in the chart, the bug would occur if the value of DELAY  was greater than 127. Due to this bug, the condition will always evaluate to false. This happens because the entire expression is incorrectly transformed to (int8_T)(modelName_DW.temporalCounter_i1) >=            modelName_P.DELAY  in the generated code. The right hand side expression, which is used to scale the internal temporal counter to the correct units of time (sec, usec,  or msec ), is incorrectly cast to a container that is too small to hold its value. This can disrupt the logic of the Stateflow chart and produce incorrect results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use event-based temporal logic operators instead of absolute time. You can manually scale your expression to evaluate to units of ticks, for example, instead of msec.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts using non-tunable struct parameters generate incorrect code
Description: When non-tunable struct parameters are used in a Stateflow chart or State Transition Table, the chart generates incorrect code which is missing initialization of the struct parameter.

Summary: Subsystems or Stateflow charts with function-call outputs may cause incorrect code generation when connected to Model blocks with function-call input ports
Description: An atomic subsystem or Stateflow chart may incorrectly generate a reused function when using Simulink Coder if all of the following apply: The subsystem or chart is outputting a function-call signal. That function-call signal is connected to a Model block input port, but not a function-call port on a Model block. There are two or more identical charts or subsystems in your model.  In this case, multiple subsystems or charts that are calling different functions will be generated as a single function, with only one of the function-calls being called from that function. ?
Workaround: Disable code reuse by setting the Function packaging parameter on the subsystem or chart to Nonreusable function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer from MATLAB? Coder? and MATLAB Function block when iterating over an array using FOR i = X syntax.
Description: When using a for -loop to iterate over an array of 3 or more dimensions using the FOR i = X  syntax, the order of the values that the generated code produces differs from the order that MATLAB produces. Consider the following example: x = cat(3, [1 3; 2 4], [5 7; 6 8]); % create some example datafor column = x    disp(column);end In MATLAB, the result is: 12345678 In the generated code, the answer is: 12563478
Workaround: Reshape the multi-dimensional array to two dimensions before the for-loop iteration.x = cat(3, [1 3; 2 4], [5 7; 6 8]); % create some example data% x(:,:) converts x to 2-dimensionalfor column = x(:,:)    disp(column); end

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Numerical mismatch between simulation and code-generation results in an export-function Model when the function-call subsystem Outport is inheriting its initial value
Description: There is a numerical mismatch in the results between simulation and code-generation when all the following conditions are true: A function-call subsystem is fed by an function-call inport at the root-level of the model An Outport block inside the Subsystem is inheriting its initial value. This can be happen through one of the following ways:- Source of initial output value  parameter of the Outport block is set to Input signal - Initial output  parameter of the Outport block is set to []  and the Output when disabled  parameter of the Outport block is set to held  The function-call subsystem's Outport block is directly connected to an Outport block at the root level of the model  The attached model demonstrates this issue.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:Place a Signal Conversion block or a Gain block with a gain of 1 as the source of the Outport block of the Function-Call SubsystemExplicitly specify a value for the Initial Output parameter of the Outport block

Summary: Cannot launch MATLAB 14a or earlier on OS X 10.10 Yosemite
Description: After installing MATLAB R2014a or earlier on a Mac running OS X 10.10 Yosemite, the application might have the prohibition symbol (a circle with a backslash) superimposed on its icon, and attempting to run the application might result in an error message, for example, 'You can't use this version of the application "MATLAB_R2014a" with this version of OS X.'

Summary: Incorrect red check using left comma separator expression
Description: Consider the following code where a comma separator is used in the condition of a if() statement: #include <stdio.h>  volatile int erd;  int foo() {  int lvar;      if (erd) {          if (erd < 4 || (lvar = 7, 0)) {              return lvar; // red check on lvar that should be green           }       }   }    Because the right expression of the logical OR is always false, the left side of the comma separator has not been evaluated. This leads to an incorrect red check on lvar .

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Incorrect simulation results in normal mode for a model referencing an export-function model which references a regular model
Description: Your model will give incorrect results in normal mode simulation if all of the following are true: A regular model (i.e. one that is not set to be an export-function model) is referenced by a Model block in an export-function model. Here, we will refer to the regular model as the bottom model and the export-function model as the mid model. The mid export-function model is referenced by a Model block in another regular model, which we will refer to as the top model. For the bottom model, Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint:  is set to Unconstrained . In the mid model, the Model block referencing the bottom model is contained in a function-call subsystem, which is driven (directly or indirectly) by a function-call root-level Inport block. We will refer to the port index of this function-call root-level Inport block in the mid model as midFcPort . In the top model, the function-call port midFcPort  of the Model block referencing the export-function mid model is driven by a function-call initiator block with a sample time different from the default sample time of the bottom model.  When all of the above conditions are met, the bottom model will not execute when simulating the top model in normal mode, yielding incorrect results.
Workaround: For the bottom model, set Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint: to Ensure sample time independent.

Summary: Code generated for model using subsystem variants might fail to compile
Description: The code generated for a model using subsystem variants might fail to compile if all these conditions are true.  The variants generate preprocessor directives. The variants are reused subsystems. The variants contain Model Reference blocks.  You might see an error similar to this one. I../slprj/ert/_sharedutils "mModelBlock7_converted_data.c" In file included from mModelBlock7_converted_data.c:16:0: mModelBlock7_converted.h:82:3: error: unknown type name 'MdlrefDW_mdlvar1_T'
Workaround: If a subsystem variant is generating preprocessor directives and contains a model block, disable its reuse by setting its Function Packaging parameter to Inline or Nonreusable function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Selector block may generate incorrect code
Description: The code generator may produce incorrect code when the following conditions exist: A model contains a Selector block whose input signal is a 2-D matrix of size nx1 . For the Selector block, the Number of input dimensions  parameter is set to 1 .  For the Selector block, the Index Option  is set to starting and ending indices (port) .   The attached example model named mSelectorBug.mdl met all the above conditions, so it generates incorrect code.
Workaround: On the block parameters dialog box for the Selector block, change the Number of input dimensions parameter from 1 to 2. For the newly added dimension, set the Index Option to Select all.?

Description: The transformation ratio, a floating point number in the range 0.001 to 2,  is truncated to an integer. A value less than 1 is truncated to 0, which results in an illegal value error since 0 is not allowed.  This error prevents the board from being set to non-integer ratios.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation and generated code results mismatch in the Math Function block for squaring for some fixed-point inputs.
Description: Simulation and code-generation give different results for some inputs to the Math Function block if the following conditions are met The block's Function  parameter is set to square  or magnitude^2 . The input and output are both fixed-point data types with Bias  set to 0. The square of the slope of the input divided by the output slope is not a power of 2. That is, ((S_in)^2/S_out) is not a power of 2.The slope can be expressed by TotalSlope  or by a combination of FixedExponent  and SlopeAdjustmentFactor .
Workaround: To work around this problem, use the Product block. Feed the two input ports of the block with the same signal to get the square output.

Summary: imwrite errors when writing TIFF image with fewer than 64 rows and more than 8192 columns
Description: When writing a TIFF image, the imwrite  function returns an error if the number of rows is less than 64 and the number of columns is greater than 8192.For example, this code:  >> im = ones([63,8193],'uint16');  >> imwrite(im, 'myfile.tif')  fails with the error:Error using wtifcTIFF library error - '_TIFFVSetField:  myfile.tif: Bad value 0 for "RowsPerStrip" tag.'Error in writetif (line 118)wtifc(data, map, filename, writemode, colorspace, required_tags);Error in imwrite (line 472)        feval(fmt_s.write, data, map, filename, paramPairs{:});
Workaround: To avoid this error, set the TIFF tag, 'RowsPerStrip', to an integer value greater than or equal to 1. For example:  >> im = ones([63,8193],'uint16');  >> imwrite(im,'myfile.tif','RowsPerStrip',1)

Summary: Print Preview Keep screen limits and ticks option can cause errors for figures with more than three axes
Description: If a figure contains more than three axes, selecting the Keep screen limits and ticks  option on the Print Preview Advanced  tab can cause an error in MATLAB. The preview window displays an error message rather than the preview image. figure;subplot(2,2,1);subplot(2,2,2);subplot(2,2,3);subplot(2,2,4);printpreview Click on the Advanced  tab and select the Keep screen limits and ticks  option.
Workaround: Instead of selecting the Keep screen limits and ticks option on the Print Preview Advanced tab, set the Axes tick mode and limits mode properties to manual.figure;subplot(2,2,1);subplot(2,2,2);subplot(2,2,3);subplot(2,2,4);Set the axes tick and limit modes to manual so they do not change.ax = findall(gcf, 'type', 'axes'); set(ax,'XTickMode','manual','YTickMode','manual','ZTickMode','manual');set(ax,'XLimMode','manual','YLimMode','manual','ZLimMode','manual');printpreviewWhen the modes are set to 'manual', it is not necessary to select theKeep screen limits and ticks option on the Advanced tab.

Summary: Exception when saving as HTML in comparison reports opened from a Simulink project
Description: In a Simulink project, if you use Compare to Ancestor  or Compare to Revision , you cannot generate a printable HTML report from the resulting comparison report. If you select Save As > Save as HTML  in the comparison report, you see an exception.
Workaround: To work around this problem, create a new comparison from the report and save that as HTML.In the comparison report opened from the Simulink Project, on the Comparison tab, click New.In the New Comparison dialog, the current comparison file names are selected by default. Click  Compare.In the new comparison report, on the Comparison tab, select Save As > Save as HTML.

Summary: Using multiple instances of MATLAB with the same IVI-C driver to connect to instruments does not work. The first instance connects fine, but subsequent instances fail to connect.
Description: Using multiple instances of MATLAB with the same IVI-C driver to connect to instruments does not work. The first instance connects fine, but subsequent instances fail to connect. The second, and any further instances of MATLAB, will throw the following error when the connect method is executed: Error using icdevice (line 233)  Failed to load shared library.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation in certain cases for functions in MATLAB code that use the same variable as input and output and modify global variables
Description: For functions that use the same argument as input and output (pass by reference argument), the generated code can be incorrect in the following case: A function directly or indirectly writes to a global variable whose value is passed to the function as an argument through a local variable copy. In the following example: fsub  uses the same variable as input and output. fsub  writes to the global g . The call to g = fsub(t)  passes the value of the global g  as an argument through a local variable t .  function y = fglobal()global g;t = g;g = fsub(t);y = g;function x = fsub(x)coder.inline('never');global g;g(1) = 2048;x = x + 1; In the following example : fsub  uses the same variable as input and output, x  fsub  writes to the global, g . The call g = fsubwrapper(t)  passes the value of the global g  as an argument through a local variable t  which is passed to the call g = fsub(t) .  function y = fglobal()global g;t = g;g = fsubwrapper(t);y = g;function y = fsubwrapper(t)global g;g = fsub(t);y = g;function x = fsub(x)coder.inline('never');global g;g(1) = 2048;x = x + 1;
Workaround: When the affected MATLAB? code is identified:To ensure that function inlining does not introduce an additional local variable copy, insert the following statement into the calling function:               coder.inline('never');Move the statement that copies the global to the local variable from the calling function to the called function.The workaround for the second example in the Description is:function y = fglobal()global g;g = fsubwrapper(g);y = g;function y = fsubwrapper(t)coder.inline('never');global g;g = fsub(t);y = g;function x = fsub(x)coder.inline('never');global g;t = g;g(1) = 2048;x = t + 1;

Summary: Closing a Stateflow machine when Find & Replace in Chart dialog box is open generates error messages
Description: You might see error messages if you do these steps: Perform an operation on a Stateflow machine using the Find & Replace in Chart dialog box. Leave the dialog box open. Close the model. Open the model and make changes.  Assuming you did not introduce modeling errors in steps 1 or 4, then you can ignore the content of the messages. ?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, close the Stateflow Find & Replace in Chart dialog box before you close the model you searched.If you do encounter this issue, to avoid additional error messages, close the Stateflow Find & Replace in Chart dialog box. ?

Summary: Find & Replace in Chart search fails when you select Replace all and Match whole word
Description: If you perform a Find and Replace in Chart search using Replace all  along with Match whole word , the operation fails and generates a Search string changed  message.
Workaround: Use one of these approaches to avoid this problem:Replace whole words one at a time.Use Replace all and the Contains word or Regular expression options (instead of Match whole word). Examine the changes carefully to confirm the changes reflect your intention when using Contains word option.?

Summary: MEX cannot find GNU gfortran compiler on Mac in MATLAB R2014a
Description: If you installed the GNU gfortran compiler in the default installation folder, /usr/local/bin , and start MATLAB R2014a from the Applications Finder, when you call the MEX command: mex -v -setup FORTRAN MATLAB displays the following error message: Verbose mode is on.... Looking for compiler 'gfortran' ...... Executing command 'which gfortran' ...No.... Looking for file '/usr/local/bin/gfortran' ...Yes.... Looking for folder '/usr/local/bin' ...Yes.... Executing command 'which gfortran' ...No.... Looking for file '/usr/local/bin/gfortran' ...Yes.... Executing command 'gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortran.dylib' ...No.Did not find installed compiler 'gfortran'.... Looking for compiler 'gfortran' ...... Executing command 'which gfortran' ...No.... Looking for file '/usr/local/bin/gfortran' ...Yes.... Looking for folder '/usr/local/bin' ...Yes.... Executing command 'which gfortran' ...No.... Looking for file '/usr/local/bin/gfortran' ...Yes.... Executing command 'gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortran.dylib' ...No.Did not find installed compiler 'gfortran'.No supported compiler or SDK was found. For options, visit "https://www.mathworks.com/support/compilers/R2014a/maci64." ? This issue does not occur if: You start the MATLAB application from the shell terminal that includes the /usr/local/bin  folder on the system PATH, which puts it in the PATH environment variable used by MATLAB. The GNU gfortran compiler installation directory exists in the PATH environment variable used by MATLAB, irrespective of the way you start the MATLAB application.  ? ?
Workaround: Choose one of the following:1. Edit the gfortran.xml file, in the matlabroot/bin/maci64/mexopts folder. Save gfortran.xml to a new file, gfortran_backup.xmlOpen gfortran.xml in a text editor.Replace the following commands:<cmdReturns name="gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortran.dylib" /><cmdReturns name="gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortranbegin.a" />           with:<cmdReturns name="$$/gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortran.dylib" /><cmdReturns name="$$/gfortran -print-file-name=libgfortranbegin.a" />2. Add the /usr/local/bin folder to the MATLAB PATH environment variable each time you run MATLAB, using the following commands:>> curPath = getenv('PATH')>> setenv('PATH', [curPath , ':/usr/local/bin'])Alternatively, add these commands to your startup.m file, located in your MATLAB startup folder, to capture this fix across multiple sessions of MATLAB.

Summary: Image Acquisition Toolbox fails to recognize the Point Grey Bumblebee2 camera in R2014a.
Description: In R2014a, Image Acquisition Toolbox does not detect the Point Grey Bumblebee2 camera using the pointgrey adaptor.

Summary: Repeated simulations of a model using model reference may fail with 'Access is denied' error
Description: Repeated simulations of a model hierarchy may fail on some hardware platforms if both these conditions exist: Multiple Model blocks in the hierarchy reference the same model. More than one of those Model blocks has Simulation mode  set to Normal  .  If a failure occurs, typically you see an error message such as this: Error writing file "C:\Users\user1\work\slprj\sim\sub_model\tmwinternal\sub_model0.slx": copy failed: Access is denied
Workaround: Change the simulation mode of Model blocks in the model hierarchy from Normal mode to Accelerator mode until at most one instance of any given model is referenced in Normal mode.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" when a vector in a Stateflow chart is assigned a value in some, but not all, branches of a condition
Description: Simulink Code Inspector returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if a Stateflow chart assigns a value to a vector on some, but not all, of the transition paths following a condition evaluation.  In this case, the traceability report traces the failure to the transitions assigning a value to that Stateflow vector.
Workaround: Update Stateflow chart so that if a vector is assigned a value on a transition path following a condition evaluation, that vector is also assigned a value on all other transition paths following the condition. For example, if a condition evaluates to true and the corresponding true transition path assigns array data v a value of 1.0, then the other transition paths should also assign a value to v according to the semantics of the state diagram.

Summary: Simulating the top model of a referenced model with empty subsystems can cause artificial algebraic loops that cannot be removed. Simulink displays a warning.
Description: If a referenced model contains empty subsystems with constant sample time, like the Model Info block, simulating the top model can create an artificial algebraic loop that contains the Model block. The loop containing the referenced model cannot be removed by checking the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences on the reference model.
Workaround: As a workaround, remove any empty subsystems in the referenced models when simulating the top model.

Summary: Simscape two-dimensional tablelookup can fail when inputs are decreasing
Description: Simscape two-dimensional lookup tables can cause a crash or give incorrect results when either of the inputs, x1d or x2d, is decreasing and the inputs are of different lengths. This bug affects both the PS Lookup Table (2D) block from the Simscape Foundation library and the tablelookup  function in the Simscape language.
Workaround: Always use strictly monotonic and increasing vectors as the inputs to the PS Lookup Table (2D) block and the Simscape language tablelookup function.

Summary: Java? Exception and abrupt exit on Mac OS X version 10.10 Yosemite
Description: When running MATLAB on Mac OS X version 10.10 Yosemite, the following Java exception is reported: java.lang.NullPointerException  at com.mathworks.widgets.WindowsWidgetFactory$SearchAndClearButton.anyText(WindowsWidgetFactory.java:187)  at com.mathworks.widgets.WindowsWidgetFactory$SearchAndClearButton.getIcon(WindowsWidgetFactory.java:195)  at com.apple.laf.AquaButtonUI.setThemeBorder(AquaButtonUI.java:113)  at com.apple.laf.AquaButtonUI.installDefaults(AquaButtonUI.java:80)  at javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicButtonUI.installUI(BasicButtonUI.java:88)  at javax.swing.JComponent.setUI(JComponent.java:664)  at javax.swing.AbstractButton.setUI(AbstractButton.java:1807)  at javax.swing.JButton.updateUI(JButton.java:146)  at javax.swing.AbstractButton.init(AbstractButton.java:2172)  at javax.swing.JButton.<init>(JButton.java:136) . . . Following this, MATLAB may exit abruptly.
Workaround: Note: If you cannot launch MATLAB at all, please see Bug Report 1116607, "Cannot launch MATLAB 14a or earlier on OS X 10.10 Yosemite".For MATLAB R2012a through R2014a only.To install this patch, you need the name and password of an account with Administrator privileges.Download the appropriate patch file for the version of MATLAB you wish to patch.Look for the file in your Downloads folder, the Desktop, or wherever your Web browser saves downloaded files.If your Web browser did not unzip the patch file for you, double-click the file in the Finder to unzip it.Double-click the .dmg file to mount the disk image.At the top level of the mounted disk image is an application such as R2014a_patch_1098655.app. Double-click to launch it.Select your MATLAB installation. Select the root folder of the install, for example, MATLAB_R2014a.app.Enter the name and password of an account with Administrator privileges.New Java class files will be installed in the java/patch/com/mathworks/widgets folder of your MATLAB installation.When the upgrade is complete, you can launch MATLAB.

Summary: For some example models, the checkbox for "measure task execution time" is greyed out.
Description: For some example models, the "measure task execution time" checkbox is greyed out. Customers are unable to enable or disable the profiling tool. Examples include xpcosc, xpccanintpcis, xpccanpcififo1s and xpccanpcis.
Workaround: 1. Open the Configuration Parameter > Code Generation pane, change the System target file from "slrt.tlc" to "grt.tlc", and click the Apply button.2. Change the System target file back to "slrt.tlc", click the Apply button, and click the OK button.3. Save the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is generated in certain cases for the initialization of a Simulink export-function model
Description: Incorrect code is generated for the initialization of a Simulink export-function model when ALL the following conditions are true: An output port of a function-call subsystem in the model is directly connected to a root-level Outport block of the model The corresponding Outport block of the function-call subsystem has multiple source blocks, which are connected to the Outport block using virtual connectivity blocks such as Mux or Bus Creator  The generated code does not initialize the root-level Outport block memory with the initial value specified in the subsystem Outport block. Instead, the root-level Outport block memory is initialized to the default value of its datatype (usually 0). This behavior can be seen by generating code from the attached models mG1095133_Mux.slx and mG1095133_VirtualBus.slx.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, one of the following steps can be taken:If the input port of the Outport block of the function-call subsystem is connected to a Mux, place a Signal Conversion block between the Mux block and the outport of the function-call subsystem.If the input port of the Outport block of the function-call subsystem is connected to a Bus-Creator block , insert a Gain block (with a Gain value of 1) between the Bus Creator Block and the outport of the function-call subsystem .The attached models mG1095133_Workaround_Mux.slx and mG1095133_Workaround_VirtualBus.slx demonstrate how this could be done.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement library setting changes may be ignored for code generation on rebuild
Description: Changes to the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Code replacement library  parameter after you generate code are ignored for future code generation when any  of these conditions apply: - The previous code generation was triggered by a Model Advisor check such as Identify questionable operations for strict single-precision design . - You used the Code Replacement Viewer to see the replacements and misses for the previous code generation.- You used the following command to examine the code replacement cache hits and misses logged during the previous code generation:  get_param(<model>,'TargetFcnLibHandle')
Workaround: After you change the Code replacement library parameter, clear the TargetFcnLibHandle parameter: set_param(<model>,'TargetFcnLibHandle',[])

Summary: Rapid Accelerator build failure for referenced models with non-inlined S-functions with parameters
Description: In Rapid Accelerator, referenced models with non-inlined S-Functions with parameters cause the top model to fail to build. You may get an error saying that the symbol mexMakeArrayPersistent  cannot be resolved.

Summary: Model Advisor Check usage of Math Operations blocks incorrectly warns if any element of a matrix defined in a Gain block contains "1"
Description: Model Advisor Check usage of Math Operations blocks  incorrectly warns if any element of a matrix defined in a Gain block contains "1". The check incorrectly assumes that if one of the matrix elements defined in a Gain block is "1", all the elements are "1".
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation in certain cases for functions that use the same variable as input and output
Description: For functions that use the same argument as input and output (pass by reference argument), the generated code can be incorrect in the following cases: Case 1:  A function directly or indirectly writes to a global variable that is passed to the function as both an input and output. In the following example: fsub  uses the same variable as input and output. fsub  writes to the global g.  The call g = fsub(g)  passes the global g  as both input and output to fsub .  function y = fglobal(u)coder.inline('never');global g;if u > 0    g = u;endg = fsub(g);y = g;function x = fsub(x)coder.inline('never');global g;g = 2048;y = x + 1; Case 2:  Array u  is a reshaped version of array m . u (n)  is passed as input to the function.The function output is assigned to m(n) . In the following example: fsub  uses the same variable as input and output. In f reshape , u  is a reshaped version of m . u(5,1)  and m(1,2)  have the same linear index, 5. The call m(1,2) = fsub(100, u(5,1))  passes u(5,1)  as input and m(1,2)  as output to fsub.   function y = freshape(u)coder.inline('never');m = makeIt(u);u = m(:);m(1,2) = fsub(100, u(5,1));y = m;function x = fsub(x,b)coder.inline('never');x = x + b;function A = makeIt(u)coder.inline('never');A = magic(4) + u; Case 3 : A handle object is passed as input to the function. Inside a call to the function, the function indirectly uses a property of that handle object to index a matrix. In the following example: fsubx  uses the same variable x  as input and output. The call [A(B.p(1)),w] = fsubx(100,B)  : Passes the handle object B  as input to fsubx.  Uses the property p  of handle object B  to index into matrix A.    function y = fraw(u)coder.inline('never');B = OneVarSizeProp(5);[A,i] = makeIt(u);w=0;if u < 100     [A(B.p(1)),w] = fsubx(100,B);endy = A + w;endfunction [x,y] = fsubx(x,B)coder.inline('never');x = x + 200;y = x - x;B.p(1) = 9;endfunction [A,i] = makeIt(u)coder.inline('never');A = (1:10) + u;i = 1 + u;endclassdef OneVarSizeProp < handle    properties        p    end    methods        function obj = OneVarSizeProp(u)            coder.inline('never');            obj.p = zeros(1,u) + u;        end    endend Case 4:  A function foo  that uses the same variable A  for an input and output calls another function foo1  that uses the same variable B  as input and output. foo1 also has an input H that is a function handle and an input C . In the call to foo1 , the variable passed to input C  is related to the variable passed to output B . For example  the call  Z = foo1(X, H1, Z ) passes the same variable  Z  for the input C  and the output B . A= foo(A)...Z = foo1(X, H1, Z)...B = foo1(B, H, C)...endend ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: XOR and XNOR blocks with constant inputs may result in incorrect code generation
Description: When using XOR or XNOR blocks, incorrect code will be generated for the model if both the following conditions are true:  The inputs to the blocks are constants. The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline parameters  option is selected.  The generated code incorrectly performs the OR and NOR operations instead of the XOR and XNOR operations respectively.
Workaround: To work around this issue, clear the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline parameters option.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation: Code replacement of fixed-point addition or subtraction operation can produce unexpected results
Description: Code replacement of a fixed-point addition or subtraction operation can produce unexpected numerical results if all of the following conditions are true: Your model has a fixed-point addition or subtraction operation with input and output types of the same wordlength (8,16, or 32) and signedness. Your code replacement library has an entry that matches the fixed-point operation with an output type that cannot represent all values of the input types (for example, if the output fraction length is more than the input fraction length).  Under these conditions the code generator incorrectly makes the code replacement and the numerical results will likely be incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect code generation for model with  MATLAB Function block and shared utilities
Description: For a model that uses the MATLAB Function block, the behavior of the generated code can differ from the behavior of the Simulink simulation when: The configuration parameter Shared code placement  is set to Shared location . In releases before R2011b, the parameter Shared code placement  (UtilityFuncGeneration ) is named Utility code generation  or Utility function generation  in the Configuration Parameters dialog box view. The model is a referenced model. A model that references a model that uses the MATLAB Function block behaves like a model that has Shared code placement  set to Shared location . If the MATLAB Function block calls the same built-in library function twice with different arguments, the coder might generate only one version of the function. Suppose that in simulation, the MATLAB Function block calls sor t  once with sort mode ascend  and once with sort mode descend . In the generated code, both sort  calls might use the same sort mode.
Workaround: If  Shared code placement is set to Shared location, change the setting to Auto:In releases R2011b and later, set Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement to Auto.In release R2011a, set Code Generation > Interface > Utility code generation to Auto.In Releases R2010a and R2010b, set Real-Time Workshop > Interface > Utility function generation to Auto.No workaround is available for the case where a model is a referenced model.

Summary: datestr and datevec return incorrect dates for serial date numbers that immediately preceed midnight
Description: The datestr  function returns values that are offset by 1 day when the input is a serial date number with a time of day that is less than 0.5 milliseconds before midnight. For example, >> datenums = 735738 + [-.001 -.0001 .0001 .001]/(24*60*60);>> ds = datestr(datenums,'dd-mmm-yyyy HH:MM:SS.FFF')ds =18-May-2014 23:59:59.99918-May-2014 00:00:00.00019-May-2014 00:00:00.00019-May-2014 00:00:00.001 datestr  rounds to the nearest millisecond. However, the date for the second value is calculated incorrectly as 18-May-2014  instead of 19-May-2014.  The datevec  function returns values that are offset by 1 day when the input is a serial date number with a time of day that is less than 0.05 milliseconds before midnight. For example, >> datenums = 735738 + [-.00001 .00001]/(24*60*60);>> datevec(datenums)ans =        2014           5          18           0           0           0        2014           5          19           0           0           0 datevec  rounds the last column (seconds) to the nearest 0.1 millisecond. However, the first value for the third column (day number) is calculated incorrectly as 18 instead of 19.
Workaround: Before calling datestr or datevec, round any affected serial date numbers up to midnight. For example,>> datenums = 735738 + [-.001 -.0001 .0001 .001]/(24*60*60);>> i = (86400 * (ceil(datenums) - datenums)) < .0005;>> datenums(i) = ceil(datenums(i));>> datestr(datenums,'dd-mmm-yyyy HH:MM:SS.FFF')ans =18-May-2014 23:59:59.99919-May-2014 00:00:00.00019-May-2014 00:00:00.00019-May-2014 00:00:00.001and >> datenums = 735738 + [-.00001 .00001]/(24*60*60);>> i = (86400 * (ceil(datenums) - datenums)) < .00005;>> datenums(i) = ceil(datenums(i));>> >> datevec(datenums)ans =        2014           5          19           0           0           0        2014           5          19           0           0           0

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is generated when the table size of an n-D Lookup Table block is tunable and code replacement library (CRL) is used to replace lookup table functions.
Description: Incorrect code is generated under the following conditions: The model contains n-D Lookup Table block with checkbox Support tunable table size in code generation  checked in the block's parameter dialog box. Code replacement library is used for replacing the generated code for the model. One or more of the functions lookup1D, lookup2D, lookup3D, lookup4D or lookup5D are replaced. A custom code replacement library entry derives the table size from the arguments of the entry and adds it to the function implementation signature.  The function signature generated will have the table size argument to be a non-tunable literal value.

Description: Several users have reported this segmentation violation when using the Simulink Library Browser.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when starting up or shutting down
Description: On rare occasions, MATLAB crashes when trying to notify the command window that the interpreter is available. This happens when the command window is not available, which typically is a result of MATLAB being in the process of starting up or shutting down. The problem usually leads to a logged stack trace, and at the top of the stack is a method called inputRequestedEventHappened .

Summary: Stateflow syntax highlighting failure

Summary: MATLAB produces a java.lang.NoSuchMethodError exception after startup or when using graphics.
Description: After startup or when using MATLAB Graphics, exceptions might display in the command window that look similar to this exception: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: javax.media.opengl.GLCapabilities.<init>(Ljavax/media/opengl/GLProfile;)V  These exceptions occur when other applications install JOGL, a Java wrapper around the OpenGL graphics library, in a Java extensions directory. The third-party JOGL software can conflict with software used by MATLAB. The class and method names that appear in the exception message vary, and depend on the JOGL version that conflicts with MATLAB. A stack follows the exception messages.
Workaround: Prevent versions of JOGL installed in Java extensions directories from conflicting with MATLAB by telling Java to not look in those extension directories.From within MATLAB, get the current value of the Java property java.ext.dirs:java.lang.System.getProperty('java.ext.dirs')Several directories are listed. One of them includes the MATLAB install directory.Create a java.opts file. If you are already using a java.opts file, edit that one. Otherwise, create one in the matlabroot/bin/arch directory. If you do not have permission to write into the matlabroot/bin/arch directory, ask your system administrator for help. For more information, see https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/java-opts-file.html.Add this line to the existing or new file:-Djava.ext.dirs=matlabextdirSpecify matlabextdir as the directory from the java.ext.dirs property that included the MATLAB install directory.Save java.opts. Then, restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Error when using the XML data file to author Model Advisor checks for model configuration parameters?
Description: The function Advisor.authoring.generateConfigurationParameterDataFile  is used to generate a XML data file for a custom Model Advisor configuration parameter check. The source  input parameter to the function can be the name of a Simulink model or a Simulink.ConfigSet  object. If Simulink.ConfigSet  object or the model uses an ERT-based system target file, Advisor.authoring.generateConfigurationParameterDataFile  errors. Custom configuration parameter checks will fail if the XML data file has self-closing XML elements to represent empty values (i.e. <value/> ).

Summary: pwelch wrongly concatenates multicolumn input
Description: When given matrix input containing information in multiple columns, older versions of pwelch  wrongly (and silently) stack the columns into a single column vector before continuing. The function now treats each column as an independent channel.
Workaround: Columns are treated as independent channels in R2014b onwards.  In R2014a and prior releases you can use a for-loop to perform the necessary computations:for i = 1:nChan  Pxx(:,i) = pwelch(x(:,i),...)end

Summary: Long MEX command generates 'Error using mex' message and fails during linking
Description: On computers running Microsoft Windows XP or later, the maximum length of the string that you can use at the command prompt is 8191 characters. This can cause issues if you use MEX to build multiple C files. For example, if the command mex ¨Cv myfun1.c myfunc2.c myfun3.c ¡­ myfunc100.c  expanded had 8191 or more characters, then MEX fails in the link step with the message Error using mex . link /nologo /manifest /DLL /EXPORT:mexFunction C:\...\myfun1.obj C:\...\myfun2.obj C:\...\myfun3.obj   Error using mex  This affects all compilers on Windows XP or later running MATLAB 2014a or later. If you think this might be your issue you can try the following diagnostic step. MEX a smaller number of files or shorten the file names, including the full Windows path, to reduce the command line argument length.  If that alleviates your issue with MEX then you might have run into the Windows command line length restriction.
Workaround: Installation InstructionsLog into an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the attached .zip file. They are mex.exe, libmexcmd.dll, and mex_builtin.dll located in \bin\win64 for 64-bit MATLAB or \bin\win32 for 32-bit MATLAB.Make a back up copy of the mexopts folder located in \bin\win64 for 64-bit MATLAB or \bin\win32 for 32-bit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file appropriate for your version of MATLAB (64-bit or 32-bit) that is associated with this Bug Report and store it in the <MATLAB> folder.Extract the contents of the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Restart MATLAB.Re-run mex on the files that previously failed.* Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see Error: Access is denied when attempting to extract the .zip file, you might need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.

Summary: Code inspection might fail to verify initialization code for model with multiple instances of referenced model
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify initialization code if the model meets the following conditions: The model contains more than one instance of a referenced model. The lexical order, by name, of the instances is different from the sorted order of the instances.
Workaround: Rename model reference instances so that the lexical order is the same as the sorted order.

Summary: Certain .fig files may not open in R2014a if they were saved in earlier releases and include polygons
Description: When a .fig  file that was created with Mapping Toolbox in a version of MATLAB earlier than R2014a is opened in R2014a, MATLAB may error and indicate that it is unable to find a callback function. This can happen if the .fig  file contains polygons. The main ways in which a polygon can be inserted into a figure are: (1) calling the geoshow  function with DisplayType 'polygon' , (2) calling the mapshow  function with DisplayType 'polygon' , (3) passing a polygon geoshape  or geostruct to geoshow , (4) passing a polygon mapshape  or mapstruct to mapshow , and (5) calling the contourfm  function.
Workaround: The updates provided in this workaround are only for release R2014a.  Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Download the attached file, workaround_1079954-14a_2014-08-05.zip, to a folder of your choosing.       On Windows double-click the file to view its contents, then click the Extract button, and extract the contents to a folder of your choosing. On Macintosh, double-click the file to extract its contents. On Linux use the unzip command.       Complete the instructions in the enclosed text file, README.txt.

Summary: Configuration Name and Description values lost when exporting a configuration set in the base workspace to a .m file
Description: When exporting a configuration set stored in the base workspace to a .m  file, the configuration Name  and Description  values are not preserved if the exported file is created in one of the following ways: Using Model Explorer to export the base workspace variable for the configuration set Calling the  matlab.io.saveVariableToScript  function Calling the deprecated Simulink.saveVars  function  A configuration set created from the .m  script will contain default values for the Name  and Description  parameters.
Workaround: In releases prior to R2014b, use the Simulink.ConfigSet.saveAs function to preserve the configuration Name and Description values in the exported .m file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Differing numeric results between generated code and MATLAB? when converting between fixed-point types, due to incorrect rounding
Description: For a function that converts a value to an intermediate fixed-point type, and then to another fixed-point type with smaller range and precision, code generated by MATLAB?  Coder? might omit the conversion to the intermediate type. This omission can result in simplified rounding behavior that differs from that of MATLAB. The following MATLAB function demonstrates the issue: function [ y ] = fi_rounding( x )    y = fi(fi(x, true, 12, 1), true, 10, 0);end If the input is a double value of 0.25, MATLAB produces 1. Code generated by MATLAB Coder produces 0. MATLAB Coder omits the conversion of x  to a fixed-point value with a width of 12 bits with one bit of fraction. Instead, it converts x  directly to a 10-bit type with no fraction bits. You can detect the incorrect code generation in two ways: Compare MATLAB results to generated MEX results. Inspect the generated code and the MATLAB code to compare them.

Summary: Use of division for fixed-point net slope computation might trigger warning or error about constant precision loss
Description: Using division for fixed-point net slope computations for net slopes that are not a power of two results in a more accurate representation of the net slope compared to the quantized fixed-point representation. The diagnostic for detecting precision loss when representing non power of two fixed-point net slopes does not take into account this improved accuracy. If all the following conditions are true, you might see a diagnostic warning or error about fixed-point constant precision loss. Data types have net slopes that are not a power of two. The Use integer division to handle net slopes that are reciprocals of integers  parameter in the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is selected. The Detect precision loss  parameter in the Diagnostics>Type Conversion  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is set to 'warning' or 'error'.

Summary: Code inspection returns "Failed to Verify" when it should return "Verified" for components containing shadowed blocks related to Data Store Memory
Description: If a model hierarchy contains both: Local  Data Store Memory, Data Store Read, or Data Store Write block(s) in a child model, AND Global  Data Store Memory, Data Store Read, or Data Store Write block(s) in a parent model with the same DataStoreName  property as those in the child model,  then Simulink Code Inspector returns "Failed to Verify" for the component when it should otherwise return "Verified".
Workaround: To work around this issue, change the DataStoreName property of the associated Data Store Memory, Data Store Read, and Data Store Write block(s) in either the parent or child model to a unique name.

Summary: SLCI may fail to verify a model if model contains mux block
Description: SLCI may fail to verify a model if the model contains mux block and the configuration option "Loop unrolling threshold" is less than the signal size of mux outport.
Workaround: Considering increasing the configuration option "Loop unrolling threshold" to be greater than the signal size of mux outport.

Summary: Code inspection might fail to verify generated code for If block containing unary minus operator

Summary: Using MATLAB Compiler on code that uses fdesign to design filter System objects throws an error
Description: Using MATLAB Compiler on code that uses fdesign  to design filter System objects throws an error. For example, if you have a file with the following code: f = fdesign.lowpass;d = design(f,'SystemObject',true); and you compile it with this command: mcc -m filename then the following error results when you run the compiled executable: Error using fdesign.abstracttype/equiripple (line 12) Undefined variable "dsp" or class "dsp.FIRFilter".  The undefined class varies depending on the type of filter design. In addition to dsp.FIRFilter , the error can occur on dsp.BiquadFilter , dsp.FIRDecimator , and similar classes.
Workaround: Recompile the code using the -a flag with the undefined class name noted in the error message.The following compiler call resolves the error for the above dsp.FIRFilter example:mcc -m filename -a dsp.FIRFilter

Summary: Calling sum, prod, max, or min along the second or a higher dimension can crash MATLAB on machines with more than 16 cores.
Description: On machines with more than 16 cores, calling sum , prod , max , or min  on a sufficiently large array along the second or a higher dimension can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can either disable multithreading in MATLAB by starting MATLAB using the -singleCompThread startup flag, or transpose or permute your array such that you can call the function along the first dimension. For example, instead of:x = rand(1000);y = sum(x, 2);Transpose your matrix before calling sum along the first dimension:y = sum(x.', 1).';

Summary: Time Scope block window in background when "Open At Simulation Start" selected
Description: If the Time Scope block window is open, it is not brought forward at the start of simulation when the "Open At Simulation Start" menu is checked.
Workaround: Double click on the scope or download the attached file and replace the ScopeBlock.m file in your MATLAB toolbox/shared/spcuilib/+scopeextensions   folder.

Summary: Setting the showhardware property does not always cause the target computer to display the supported Ethernet cards
Description: When Graphics mode is enabled in Simulink Real-Time Explorer, step 10 in Ethernet Card Selection by Index does not display the supported Ethernet cards on the target computer monitor.
Workaround: Follow step 7 in Ethernet Card Selection by Index to avoid this issue: "7.Select Target settings and clear the Graphics mode check box. This setting causes the kernel to print text only."

Summary: Calling coder.updateBuildInfo in a System Object with boolean parameter values may cause an error
Description: Calling coder.updateBuildInfo  in a System Object with boolean parameter values may cause the following error:  Incorrect arguments have been passed to: RTW.BuildInfo.addLinkObjects Please refer to the help on this method for more information. An example of a call that error out is: coder.updateBuildInfo('addLinkObjects',libName, libPath, '', true, false);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement library generates incorrect code for blocks involving inputs with heterogenous fixed-point types
Description: When all of the following conditions exist, the code generator can replace code for a function incorrectly: You use Embedded Coder to generate code for a Simulink model. You configure the model for the code generator to apply a code replacement library. The library specifies replacement of a scalar function (for example, min , max ) that takes scaled, fixed-point data for input arguments. Simulink block that is triggering the code replacement has inputs with heterogenous, scaled fixed-point types  The bug can appear in the generated code as an incorrect numerical value, function argument, or unwanted right shift operation on the function argument, within the replaced function. ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, identify blocks in the model that would generate a fixed-point function call for which you have registered a replacement in the code replacement library and that have input signals that do not have homogeneous fixed-point scaling. Then, use Data Type Conversion blocks to explicitly convert the data types of the signals to be homogeneous.?

Summary: loadlibrary is incompatible with code generated by Simulink Embedded Coder in R2014a
Description: Suppose you use Simulink Embedded Coder to generate code for a model that selects an embedded target (for example, the ERT shared library target). If you use loadlibrary  to load the dll  file produced by compiling that code, MATLAB throws an error that various typedefs and macros associated with builtin types are redefined. For example, you have a Simulink model named 'SharedLibrary' and you generated the following dll , DLL  = 'SharedLibrary_win64'; When you try to load the dll  with the loadlibrary  command, loadlibrary(DLL,'SharedLibrary_ert_shrlib_rtw\SharedLibrary.h',...'mfilename','SharedLibrary_Import');  MATLAB R2014a fails to load the dll  with the following error, Warning: Warnings messages were produced while parsing. Check the functions you intend to use for correctness. Warning text can beviewed using:[notfound,warnings]=loadlibrary(...) > In loadlibrary at 347In SharedLibrary_UT at 29 Error loading library intermediate output follows.
Workaround: Select one of the following workarounds:Use a non-embedded target when generating code for the model.Install attached file to patch loadlibrary.To install the patch:Download the attached file to your desktop.In MATLAB execute: cd([matlabroot '\toolbox\matlab\general\private'])Backup the file prototypes.pl.Extract prototypes.pl from the .zip file to this folder.Run your original code.?

Summary: MATLAB may crash when attaching/detaching external displays on a Mac
Description: Due to a bug in the version of Java used by MATLAB, MATLAB may crash when plugging or unplugging external monitors or projectors on the Mac operating system. Sometimes the crash will happen only when trying to modify windows within the MATLAB program after attaching/detaching these kinds of external displays.  These work-flows cause MATLAB to crash generating a crash log that contains a line with the following: __Java_sun_lwawt_macosx_CWrapper_00024NSObject_release_block_invoke_1  If the crash log does not contain this line, the cause of the crash may be different.
Workaround: To workaround this crashing behavior, you would need to have MATLAB utilize a version of Java that does not have this issue.  To do so follow these steps:1.  Install Java Runtime 7u45 or newer (but not the forthcoming Java 8) from Oracle¡¯s web site.  You can utilize the link below to download the Java Runtime 7u55:Oracle Java 7u552.  Change the version of Java that MATLAB is using as described at:How do I change the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) that MATLAB is using for Mac OS?Further Background:R2013a and previous releases of MATLAB for Mac used Java that comes bundled with OS X; however, starting with MATLAB R2013b, MathWorks migrated to Oracle's Java 7 instead of the one bundled with OS X. This is to ensure the continued availability of MATLAB on Mac computers because Apple is in the process of deprecating its Java support.R2013b is bundled with Java 7u11. This version has a problem with dynamic change of displays as described in the following web page.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global data store memory referenced across model reference boundary
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are met: You defined a data store in the base workspace and it is referenced across a model reference boundary. The references of the data store in the child model and the parent model have data dependency across the model boundary. In the generated code of the parent model, optimization of the references of the data store is desirable.  For an example, see the attached model m1072759.slx . In the generated code, notice that myDSM  is reused incorrectly.
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB script WorkaroundForBugReport1072759.p.To enable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport1072759(true).To disable the workaround, execute the script WorkaroundForBugReport1072759(false).

Heartbleed exposes a vulnerability in OpenSSL (a widely used cryptographic library that supports private communications across the Internet). OpenSSL is used in MATLAB Production Server (MPS) for the optional support of the HTTPS protocol for secure client-server communication. If you are using or intend to use HTTPS with MPS R2013b+ or R2014a, we encourage you to apply the appropriate patch below to ensure your work data is secure.

Download the appropriate .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MPSROOT> folder. glnxa64 indicates Linux 64-bit, maci64 indicates Mac OS 64-bit, and win32 and win64 indicate Windows 32-bit and Windows 64-bit installations respectively.                     Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MPSROOT> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MPSROOT>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MPSROOT> folder.  *Notes for Windows Systems:      If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.    Extract  the contents of the file to your <MPSROOT> folder.

Summary: Possible MATLAB? exception with code generation for mutiple entry-point functions when at least one of the entry-point functions uses a constant global variable
Description: You can generate code for multiple entry-point functions. Each entry-point function can use different sets of global variables. If at least one of the entry-point functions uses constant global variables, code generation for multiple entry-point functions can cause  a MATLAB exception. This bug does not affect code generation for a single entry-point function that uses constant global variables.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variable transport delay and variable time delay with vector inputs may not support Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator modes and RSim target.
Description: The variable transport delay and variable time delay with vector inputs may not support Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator modes and RSim target, if the length of the vector is longer than the "Loop unrolling threshold" value set in the 'Signals and Parameters' panel in Model Configuration Parameters->Optimization. In this case, Simulink displays this error : error: 'u1' undeclared (first use in this function)'. ? ?
Workaround: Using the UI: In Model Configuration Parameterscon->Optimization->Signals and Parameters, change the "Loop Unrolling Threshold" to a value larger than the length of the input vector of the Variable Transport/Time Delay blocks.Using the command line: In the command line, run this command set_param(modelName, 'RollThreshold', N) where N is a number larger than the length of the input vector of the Variable Transport/Time Delay blocks

Summary: Unexpected mask parameter type causes build failure.
Description: When a Saturate block with an input parameter of type real  is provided with an integral limit, there can be build failures.
Workaround: When the integral limit is defined by a simulink parameter of integral type using subsystem mask mechanism, change the type of the simulink parameter to real.

Summary: Linker error LNK1104 when using MEX to rebuild existing MEX-file in a directory with spaces
Description: Suppose you create a MEX-file in a directory with spaces in the name and then call it to load it into memory. Calling MEX again to rebuild the function leads to a linker error as part of an attempt to overwrite the existing MEX-file. To reproduce the error, create a directory with a name that has a space in it (for example, C:\Work\test dir ) and cd  to that directory in MATLAB. cd('C:\Work\test dir')!cp (matlabroot)\extern\examples\mex\explore.c . dd = pwd; ff = fullfile(dd, 'explore.c'); mex('-outdir', dd, ff) explore 15 mex('-outdir', dd, ff) Building with 'Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 (C)'. Error using mex LINK : fatal error LNK1104: cannot open file 'C:\Work\test dir\explore.mexw64'
Workaround: Clear the MEX-file before running the MEX command again.ff = fullfile(dd, 'explore.c');mex('-outdir', dd, ff)explore 15clear mexmex('-outdir', dd, ff)

Summary: imfilter returns wrong output for flat images on certain processors
Description: A flat image is an image whose pixels all have the same intensity value. When you pass a flat image along with certain kernels as input to the imfilter  function, the function should return a flat image as an output. However, for certain types of processors (AVX capable processor) and for a small subset of kernels, imfilter  does not return a flat output image. To check if the bug exists on your machine, execute the following: I = 4*ones(5); x = -3:3; hx = -x.*exp(-(x.*x)/(4.5)); hx = hx/sum(hx(1:4)); hx(abs(hx)<eps*max(abs(hx(:)))) = 0; Gx = imfilter(I,hx','replicate') isequal(numel(unique(Gx)),1) If the output of the code snippet shown above is 1, the processor you are using does not have this bug. If the output of the code snippet shown above is 0, follow the workaround.
Workaround: The workaround is to use the following command before calling the imfilter function.iptsetpref('UseIPPL', 0)?

Summary: Unable to access parameters of the referenced subsystem when the top model contains only model reference blocks
Description: Unable to access parameters from a Simulink Real-Time application built from a top model wired for concurrency with only model  reference blocks representing the work tasks and no non-model-reference blocks in the top model. Trying to view the parameters from the Simulink Real-Time Explorer or to access the parameters from the command line results in the following error: "Invalid parameter index"
Workaround: As a Workaround please contact tech-support to obtain the fix for this issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Transpose of a two-dimensional variable-size matrix causes a size mismatch error on the first dimension
Description: The transpose of a two-dimensional variable-size matrix can cause the following MEX run-time error: Size mismatch error on dimension 1: expected 100, but actual size is 1.   The dimensions of the matrix are unknown, but the MEX code  determines that the first dimension can be set to 1.
Workaround: Try to replace  the transposes that are size 1 by-:? or :?-by-1 with calls to reshape.

Summary: Double-click on a block that links to missing library fails
Description: The following sequence of actions crash MATLAB: Open a model with a block that references a missing library. Fix the link issue. Without closing the Simulink Editor, double-click the block that referenced the missing library.  ?
Workaround: After fixing a bad library link, right-click on the block (instead of double-clicking). From the context menu, select Block Parameters to bring up the parameters dialog box.

Summary: On Mac ".mdlp" extension is appended to downloaded Adobe files
Description: Mac customers with MATLAB R2012b through R2014a installed might find that when downloading some kinds of Adobe documents using their web browser, the file extension ".mdlp" is automatically appended to the filename. For example, a ".dmg" disk image file might be saved with the extension ".dmg.mdlp". This prevents the document from being used as intended on the Mac. For example, you cannot double-click a file with the extension ".dmg.mdlp" to mount it as a disk image.
Workaround: To fix this issue in an R2012b, R2013a, R2013b, or R2014a installation, you will need the name and password of an account with Administrator privileges.Download the patch file "patch_1067865.dmg.zip" attached to this report.Locate the download.If your web browser did not unzip it for you, double-click it in the Finder to unzip the file.Double-click "patch_1067865.dmg" to mount the disk image.At the top level of the mounted disk image is an application called "patch_1067865.app." Double-click to launch it.Select your MATLAB installation. Select the root folder of the install, for example, "MATLAB_R2014a.app".Enter the name and password of an account with Administrator privileges.When the upgrade is complete, restart your computer.The patch updates the "Info.plist" file in the "Contents" folder of your MATLAB installation, saving the previous version as "Info.plist.old".

Summary: 'Search For All References' changes location in source code
Description: During a results review, when you right-click a variable or function name and select 'Search For All References', you lose your current location on the 'Source' pane. Both on the 'Source' and 'Search' pane, the first occurrence of the variable or function name is highlighted.

Description: Uncompilable C API code may be generated when both these conditions exist: A signal is test-pointed at the output of a Bus Selector or a Selector block operating on a nonvirtual bus. The test-pointed signal is within a model referenced in Accelerator mode.

Summary: audiowrite converts floating-point data to int16 incorrectly
Description: The audiowrite  function scales the conversion of floating-point data to int16  incorrectly when writing FLAC and WAV files.  When you read the audio file back into MATLAB?, some values of the data can be offset by one bit. This does not result in any audible difference in the file.  The offset occurs when you call audiowrite  like this:audiowrite(filename, data, 'BitsPerSample', 16); where data  is the floating-point audio data written to the file called filename . The offset occurs only when data  contains values greater than 0.5 or less than -0.5 (the limits for floating-point audio data).
Workaround: Use wavread instead.

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for model containing a Polynomial block with identical coefficients
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Polynomial block. The coefficients of the Polynomial block are all identical. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization  pane, the Block reduction  check box is selected. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, the Loop unrolling threshold  parameter is set to a value less than the input signal dimensions.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, set the Loop unrolling threshold parameter to a value greater than the input signal dimensions.

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for model containing Multiport Switch block with constant vector input
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Multiport Switch block. The data port of the Multiport Switch block is connected to a Constant block generating a vector with all identical values.
Workaround: Consider removing the Multiport Switch block and use a Constant block generating the value directly.

Summary: Saturable Transformer block has corrupted parameters in copied version
Description: When you open a copied version of a Saturable Transformer block, the dialog box parameters are missing or are inconsistant with the parameters in the original version of the block in the powerlib library.
