Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Delay balancing incorrect for control signals of enabled subsystems
Description: Delay balancing may fail to insert delays on the control signals of enabled subsystems, which can cause a simulation mismatch between the generated model and the original model.  For example, consider an enabled subsystem with a control signal input and a data signal input. If the data signal is driven by another block that has one output pipeline delay, delay balancing should insert a delay on the control signal to synchronize the inputs to the enabled subsystem. However, no delay is added on the control signal.

Summary: Segmentation violation during partial loading of library containing mask-on-mask blocks.
Description: A segmentation violation might occur when you open a model that contains blocks from a library that also contains mask-on-mask blocks. When you open such a model, the library can get partially loaded and causes the segmentation violation.
Workaround: Locate the library, open the mask-on-mask blocks, and delete the top mask so that the mask-on-mask blocks are converted into regular masked blocks. If you require the second mask on these blocks, add them to your model as linked blocks and then add mask on the linked block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced model is not rebuilt when the value of a variable it uses is changed
Description: When the value of a variable is inlined in the generated code for a referenced model, changing the value of this variable sometimes does not cause the referenced model to be rebuilt as expected. This issue happens when all of the following conditions are satisfied: The variable is not explicitly configured to be tunable, i.e. it is either a plain MATLAB variable or a Simulink.Parameter object with Auto storage class. The variable is referenced by a block parameter but such reference is not preserved. One example of such case is "2^Var". Instead of the variable "Var", the block parameter (e.g. "Gain_Gain") that contains this expression is preserved in the generated code and is initialized with the value of "2^Var". The parameter Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Inline parameters  for the referenced model is turned off.  ?

Summary: DOA estimators may return fewer number of angles than requested.
Description: Customer can specify the number of signals to be detected by setting the NumSignals  property in the following DOA estimators: phased.BeamscanEstimator , phased.BeamscanEstimator2D , phased.BeamspaceESPRITEstimators , phased.ESPRITEstimator , phased.MVDREstimator , phased.MVDREstimator2D , phased.RootMUSICEstimator , and phased.RootWSFEstimator . In rare cases, such as when noise is not present, these estimators may not be able to find the specified number of peaks in the spectrum and may return a fewer number of angles.

Summary: MATLAB might slow down significantly when SimEvents compiles a model containing Replicate or Entity Splitter blocks with a large numbers of output ports
Description: MATLAB might consume a large amount of memory and become extremely slow when SimEvents compiles a model containing either a Replicate block or an Entity Splitter block that meets both of these conditions: The entity output parameter of the block is set to Any entity output port is available . The block has many output ports that are ultimately connected to queues or servers that block entities when their storage is full.   The specific number of output ports on a Replicate block or a Entity Splitter block that causes this problem to occur depends both on the overall number of entity paths in your model and the amount of memory available on your computer.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:If appropriate, change the entity output parameter of the Replicate block or Entity Splitter block to All entity output ports are available. This configuration greatly reduces the number of calculations (and hence memory usage) performed within SimEvents to track distinct entity paths.Attempt to modify storage blocks connected directly to the Replicate block or Entity Splitter block to have infinite storage (e.g. use an Infinite Server block or specify a queue of infinite capacity). This will reduce the memory required by SimEvents to keep track of distinct entity paths.

Summary: Incorrect display of mask contents due to TeX formatting commands in block masks
Description: If you create a custom mask on a Simulink block and use one of the following Tex formatting commands in its content, the mask may be drawn incorrectly onscreen.  \bf (bold)  -- has no effect. \fontname{courier new}  -- (or other monospaced font) may result in overlapping characters if you are relying on the monospacing to align columns of text.  ?

Summary: Incorrect code replacement can occur for addition operations on complex types
Description: Incorrect code replacement can occur when the following conditions exist: Two addition operation replacement entries are registered in specified code replacement library (CRL). One addition replacement entry operates on complex types and the other operates on real types. The base type of the complex types specified for one replacement entry match the the real types for the other replacement entry. Your model includes a Sum block that you configured with a single input signal. The input data type for the Sum block matches the data type specified in the code replacement entry that specifies complex types.  Under these conditions, the code generator uses the entry that specifies the real data types instead of the entry that specifies the complex types when replacing code.
Workaround: If your model includes Sum blocks and you use a code replacement library that specifies replacement entries for addition operations on complex types, configure the Sum blocks with multiple input signals.  You can examine the contents of a code replacement library by using the Code Replacement Viewer.

Summary: MATLAB may crash while working with nested masked library blocks
Description: When looking under a mask, MATLAB may crash if all of the following conditions are true. If you right-click a library block and select Mask > Look Under Mask. The library block has one or more child library blocks. The parent block performs a set_param operation on the child block as part of this Look Under Mask operation, for example, in the parent block's initialization code.  The crash may also occur when first loading the parent library if its mask also has icon drawing commands. The crash report will include a reference to libmwsimulink.dll, for example: [  0] 0x0000000104b8af89 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+08499081 SLGlue::GraphListener::~GraphListener+00003321
Workaround: To work around this issue, please download the attachment and extract the script file attachment_1062704_12a_through_14a_2014_08_04.p to a folder on your MATLAB search path.To enable the workaround, run the script in your MATLAB session before working with nested masked library blocks.To disable the workaround, restart MATLAB.

Summary: MEX code performance degradation from additional memory integrity checks
Description: For MEX code generation with memory integrity checking enabled, MATLAB?  Coder? version R2014a (2.6) might generate memory integrity checks that were not present when using version R2013b (2.5). These additional checks are for built-in MATLAB functions. They can cause MEX code performance degradation. ? ?
Workaround: To improve the performance of MEX code that contains additional memory integrity checks from MATLAB Coder R2014a:Test the MEX code (with memory integrity checking) in MATLAB until you are satisfied that the MEX code is error-free.Regenerate the MEX code with memory integrity checking disabled.To disable memory integrity checking:In a project, on the Project Settings dialog box Speed tab, clear the Ensure memory integrity check box.At the command line interface, set the MEX configuration object IntegrityChecks property to false.       mexcfg = coder.config('mex');       mexcfg.IntegrityChecks = false;??

Summary: MATLAB Function block asserts for non-tunable parameter with size and type mismatch.
Description: If you specify the type and size of a non-tunable parameter in a MATLAB Function block, you will get the following assertion if both the type and size do not exactly match the parameter value:failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\base_core\value\staticmatrixvaluecg.cpp at line 63:baseType->equals(aSrcValue->type())  The block is designed to convert the constant if the sizes and types are compatible, or produce an error message if not.

Summary: Models containing user-provided Simulink? enumerations may fail to simulate in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: A model that contains a Simulink enumeration class that specifies a header file and whose DataScope  is not set to 'Exported'  may fail to simulate in rapid accelerator mode due to the following assertion: CGIR assertion 'index>=0 && index<fSize' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\util_prim\cg_assert.cpp:38'
Workaround: The user may work around this issue by changing the DataScope of their enumerated types to 'Auto' and by not specifying a header file for the enumerated type while simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode.

Summary: C compiler error for MATLAB Coder MEX function generation on Apple Mac platforms that use Xcode version 5.0 or higher
Description: In R2013a, R2013b, and R2014a, MATLAB? Coder? fails when compiling on Apple Mac computers with Xcode version 5.0 or higher. The compilation fails because Xcode version 5.0 (and higher) does not include the Mac OS X 10.7 SDK required by MATLAB Coder. The error message includes "macosx10.7" similar to the following:SDK macosx10.7 cannot be located
Workaround: Follow the steps in https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/103904.

Summary: mex command to build a binary from source file without exports displays unnecessary output to MATLAB command line due to mex trying to delete non-existing *.exp file
Description: Using mex  command with multiple C source files tries to delete temporary files more then once and fails if an already deleted file is requested to be deleted a second or third time. This results in unwanted message output. For example,  Could Not Find C:\Users\myname\myfun_wrapper.exp This error message is misleading and does not impact the mex build process.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when performing Windows file operations on certain file types
Description: MATLAB may crash when performing Windows operations involving OPC (Open Packaging Convention) files such as SLX, MLAPPINSTALL or SLDD files. This can include command line file operations, using file Save dialogs from MATLAB or Simulink, or interacting with files from the Current Folder browser.Crashes may occur involving file operations under the following conditions: If R2014a and a later release are installed locally, then R2014a may crash. If R2014a is installed locally, and a later release is run from a network location, then the later release may crash. If a release after R2014a is installed locally and R2014a is run from a network location, then R2014a may crash.  The crash report will include a reference to propertyimp.dll, for example: [ 24] 0x00000000546c6927 C:\ProgramData\MathWorks\R2014b\ShellExtensions\bin\win64\propertyimp.dll+00026919
Workaround: To workaround this issue, install the attached patch on R2014a.  This will prevent crashes resulting from this issue in both R2014a and later releases. Please note that you will have to copy files to both matlabroot and C:\ProgramData\MathWorks\ (see step 8).Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the appropriate attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. For a 64 bit machine, use only the zip file with win64 in its name; for a 32 bit machine, use only the zip file with win32 in its name.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Copy property_handler.dll and propertyimp.dll to C:\ProgramData\MathWorks\R2014a\ShellExtensions\bin\<arch> where <arch> is win64 for 64 bit machines and win32 for 32 bit machines. Note that this is in C:\ProgramData\MathWorks\ and not your <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log: May 8, 2014: attachment_1059612_14a_win32_2014-05-6.zip, attachment_1059612_14a_win64_2014-05-6.zip: initial version, works on R2014a only

Summary: Generated code for the Sqrt block unnecessarily resets solver at each major time step.
Description: When running in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator, generated code for the Sqrt block (with default settings) unnecessarily resets solver at each major time step.For some solvers, especially ODE15s, it forces the time step remain very small due to the continuous reset and ultimately the Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator modes take as much a 100x more steps and runs much slower than normal mode.

Summary: engGetVariable does not work with data types other than double and char on Linux and Mac in MATLAB R2014a
Description: In MATLAB R2014a, the MATLAB engGetVariable()  Engine API function displays the following error when used with variables other than double  and char  on Linux and Mac:  Error using save Can't write file stdio. For example, the following Engine application code, which returns a boolean variable from MATLAB, can trigger the error: engEvalString(ep, "b=true");engGetVariable(ep, "b");
Workaround: There is no workaround for this issue.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify S-function blocks created with Legacy Code Tool
Description: When the Legacy Code Tool specification structure's .OutputFcnSpec  lacks spaces around the equals sign, as it does in the following example:  lct_spec = legacy_code( 'initialize' );   lct_spec.OutputFcnSpec = 'double y1=function( double u1 )';   the Simulink Code Inspector verification will fail, resulting in a false negative in the code verification report. When the Legacy Code Tool specification structure's .OutputFcnSpec  lacks output, as it does in the following example:  lct_spec = legacy_code( 'initialize' );   lct_spec.OutputFcnSpec = 'function( double u1 )';   the Simulink Code Inspector verification will fail, resulting in a false negative in the code verification report.
Workaround: Add spaces around the equals sign in the .OutputFcnSpec field such as:lct_spec = legacy_code( 'initialize' );lct_spec.OutputFcnSpec = 'double y1 = function( double u1 )'; And then re-generate the S-function wrapper, ".mex" file, ".tlc", etc.Add void in the .OutputFcnSpec field such as:lct_spec = legacy_code( 'initialize' );lct_spec.OutputFcnSpec = 'void function( double u1 )'; And then re-generate the S-function wrapper, ".mex" file, ".tlc", etc.

Summary: Use of MATLAB Compiler with code containing calls to fdesign.bandpass, fdesign.bandstop, or fdesign.arbmag throws an error.
Description: Use of MATLAB Compiler with code containing calls to fdesign.bandpass , fdesign.bandstop , or fdesign.arbmag  yields one of the following errors:  Undefined function or variable 'fdesign' or 'fdesign.bandpass'.  Warning: an error occurred while parsing class fspecs.bpweightconstrained:Undefined variable "spcuddutils" or class "spcuddutils.addpostsetprop".
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached file.Open Instructions.txt and follow the directions.NOTE: This fix works only for releases R2011b, R2012a, R2012b, R2013a, and R2013b.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Binary-point multiplication or division with a small input constant may generate incorrect code
Description: This bug may result in incorrect generated code. This bug is present for MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function Block in R2014a only. It is present for Simulink Blocks and Stateflow starting in R2008b. All  the following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: A binary-point multiplication or division with a signed input constant with 2-bit word length or unsigned input constant with 1-bit word length The net scaling must be a power of 2 and not equal to 1. For example, the net scaling for sfix2 / sfix10 = sfix32_En24 is 2^24.
Workaround: For signed inputs, change the word length of the input constant from 2 bits to 3 bits. For unsigned inputs, change the word length of the input constant from 1 bit to 2 bits.For example, consider the following MATLAB Code:a = fi(1, 1, 2, 0);b = fi( 136 + u, 1, 10, 0);nt = numerictype(1,32,24); y = divide(nt, a, b );To avoid this bug, change the construction of 'a' to:a = fi(1, 1, 3, 0);

Summary: Code generation fails with internal error: CGIR assertion 'var != 0'
Description: Code generation for a model fails with an internal error when all of the following conditions are true:  A mux or bus signal is connected to the input of an Add block. A code replacement library entry exists for the addition operation and that entry results in a replacement. The replacement function requires the mux or bus signal to be aligned as specified in the code replacement entry.  Under these conditions, the software displays an error that includes the following text: Code generation fails with internal error: CGIR assertion 'var != 0'
Workaround: Do one of the following:Replace the mux or bus signal with a vector signal. For example, use a Vector Concatenate block, which concatenates input signals to create an output signal with elements residing in contiguous locations in memory.Break the direct connection between the Add block and the mux or bus signal. For example, pass the mux or bus signal through a Gain block with the gain set to 1.0.

Summary: Performance Advisor validates accuracy even when some signals are outside tolerance.
Description: Performance Advisor provides an option to validate simulation accuracy with every check. However, if the baseline measurement of a model logs multiple signals, as long as the last signal logged in Simulink Data Inspector is within its tolerance limits for accuracy, Performance Advisor can report successful validation even if other signals are not within their tolerance limits.
Workaround: 1. Download the utilCompareBaseLine zip file that corresponds to the version of MATLAB installed on your system. 2. Unzip the file. 3. Copy utilCompareBaseLine.p to this directory: matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/performance/performancea/private When doing so, overwrite the existing utilCompareBaseLine.p file.

Summary: Code inspection gives incorrect results for referenced models using function prototype control.
Description: Simulink Code Inspector may incorrectly report a verification failure for referenced models that use function prototype control for the model step function.
Workaround: Disable function prototype control and use default model initialize and step functions for Function specification.

Summary: Code generation fails for ODE solvers when events function returns scalar
Description: During code generation, MATLAB?  can crash with the following assertion failure:   Assertion at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\util_cg\sanitycheckerimpl.cpp line 1581: ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: StaticMatrixType::isa(aExpr->type()) == StaticMatrixType::isa(aExpr->rhs()->type())  The following conditions can cause this assertion failure: You call an ODE solver such as ode45 . You define an options structure and pass it to the solver as its fourth (optional) argument. You specify events location properties in that options structure as the Events  option. The function that you associate with the Events  option returns a scalar for its value  output parameter.  For example: function [t, y, stats] = myFunction(a, b)s = odeset('Events', myEventHandler);y0 = [0, 1, 1, 1];[t, y, stats] = ode45(@f, [a,b], y0, s);function yp = f(t, y)yp = y;function [value, isterminal, direction] = myEventHandler(t, y)isterminal = false;direction = zeros('like', t);value = y(1) - y(2);
Workaround: If you defined an events function that returns a scalar value, try extending the value with an additional event that will not affect the results. For example, modify the above example as follows:function [value, isterminal, direction] = myEventHandler(t, y)isterminal = false(2, 1); direction = zeros(2, 1, 'like', t);value = [y(1) - y(2); 1];

Summary: Wrong setting for signed right shift when using Polyspace for MBD
Description: The 'Signed right shift' option specifies whether signed right shifts are arithmetical or logical. In Polyspace for MBD, this option is badly managed and may cause wrong right shift operations in the Polyspace results (a logical shift is used instead of an arithmetic one).

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation and code generation mismatch when a Constant block drives a Rate Transition block through nested reusable subsystem and model boundaries.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when all of the following conditions are met: It contains a Model block that is driving a Rate Transition block. In the model referenced by the Model block, a Constant block nested inside a reusable subsystem is driving the Outport corresponding to the connection to the Rate Transition block. In the Rate Transition block, the input sample time is greater than the output sample time and the option Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is unchecked. When the model is updated, the icon of the Rate Transition block shows the word No Op .  With this bug, the Rate Transition block will output the value of the Constant block at the first sample hit in simulation, while the same Rate Transition block will output zero at the first sample hit in the generated code.
Workaround: In the Rate Transition block, check the option Ensure data integrity during data transfer and specify a value for the parameter Initial condition.

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for model with cascaded Polynomial blocks
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if both of the following conditions exist: The model contains cascaded Polynomial blocks. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, the Optimize global data access  parameter is set to None .
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, set Optimize global data access to Use global to hold temporary results or Minimize global data access.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Mismatch between behavior of simulation and code when model contains lookup table operating on fixed-point data
Description: You might experience a mismatch between simulation and code behaviors if you are working with a model that contains a lookup table that operates on fixed-point data. This mismatch in behaviors occurs when both these conditions are met. Some parameters of the lookup table block are tunable. Fixed-point attributes such as scaling, width, signedness, or bias are different for the signals and tunable parameters on which the block operates.  Prior to R2012b, Simulink detected these conditions and reported an error during code generation.
Workaround: If you are working with a model that contains a lookup table block that operates on fixed-point data, check for these conditions in the model.Some parameters of the lookup table block are tunable.Fixed-point attributes such as scaling, width, signedness, or bias are different for the signals and tunable parameters on which the block operates.If both these conditions are satisfied, set the storage class of the tunable parameters to auto, in order to ensure that these parameters are not tunable in the generated code.

Summary: Simulation target code generated for inactive variants in Accelerator mode

Summary: phased.ReceiverPreamp output does not depend on the NoiseBandwidth property value
Description: The output of step() method of phased.ReceiverPreamp does not depend on the NoiseBandwidth property. The value of this property is not required for the computation. The customer should not need to specify this property.

Summary: The showExtrinsics function incorrectly renders the camera-centric view for stereo.
Description: The showExtrinsics  function incorrectly renders camera 2 on the wrong side of camera 1. This incorrect display occurs when you call the function on a stereoParameters  object, using the camera-centric view.
Workaround: To avoid the problem in the future, download the attached showExtrinsics.zip file and follow these steps:Uncompress the zip file.Copy the showExtrinsics.m file into the <MATLABROOT>\toolbox\vision\en folder. <MATLABROOT> can be obtained by running the matlabroot function.If prompted whether to replace existing files, click yes.After copying the files, restart MATLABAfter this change, the showExtrinsics function correctly renders camera 2.

Summary: Generating code for a model which references a protected model can result in an error in the before_tlc hook function
Description: When generating code for a model that references a protected model, an error occurs when the following conditions exist: The build process has been customized to have a before_tlc  hook function. The before_tlc  hook function calls set_param  or some other method that changes the model being built.  The error may be similar to this: Cannot modify parameter 2 of S-function 'masterAppl/S-Function1' while simulation is running

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If a model sets TLC variables to allow CPU overloads and allows parallel execution at different sample rates, the model fails to build.
Description: The following options are incompatible: Allowing CPU overloads by setting the TLC option model parameter. Choosing parallel execution of different rate groups.  The initial model build check fails. To work around this incompatiblity, you must configure the model to use model reference and move the different rate groups to separate referenced models.
Workaround: To work around this problem you need to modify your model. There are two possibilities:In the same model, allow either CPU overloads or parallel execution of different rate groups, but not both.Partition the model to separate the CPU overload TLC option and the parallel execution setting from the part of the model that runs on the target computer, and partition the parts of the model that run with different rates.To partition the model, do the following:Create a new top level model and insert a model reference block that refers to your original model.In the new top level model, set the TLC option to allow CPU overloads and select parallel execution. Partition the model reference block (your original model) so that partitions with different rates run as different reference models?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Differing numeric results between generated code and simulation when converting between fixed-point types, due to incorrect rounding
Description: When a Simulink?  model converts a value to an intermediate fixed-point type, and then to another fixed-point type with smaller range and precision, code generation sometimes omits the conversion to the intermediate type. This omission can result in simplified rounding behavior that differs from the simulation rounding behavior. This bug can occur when adjacent data type conversion blocks have output data types that are fixed-point types. This bug can also occur with other blocks that operate on fixed-point types. You can detect  incorrect code generation in two ways: Compare simulation results to code generation results. For instructions, see this answer on MATLAB Central . Inspect the generated code and the model to compare them.

Summary: Global variable gets wrong value when directly assigned the input by an Interpreted MATLAB Function block
Description: When a global variable is used in a MATLAB function called by an Interpreted MATLAB Function block, its value can be incorrectly changed if the following conditions are true. The global variable is directly assigned the input value The global variable is not written since the assignment  For example, suppose an Interpreted MATLAB Function block calls the following MATLAB function. function UserDefinedFunction(input)    global aGlobalVariable;    if input == 0        aGlobalVariable = input;    endend If the block input is 0 at time step 0 and 1 at time step 1, aGlobalVariable  becomes 1 after time step 1, which is wrong.
Workaround: To work around this problem, do not directly assign the input value to a global variable. Instead, change the right side of the assignment to an expression like 'aGlobalVariable = input + 0;'.

The model contains a block that is linked to a library. 3. The library block uses a mask whose initialization code deletes a block underneath the mask.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation software does not report an error when a program uses a persistent variable before assigning it a value
Description: In certain cases, the code generation software does not report an error when a program uses a persistent variable before assigning it a value. This problem can occur when the following conditions are true: A persistent variable q  is initialized only when another persistent variable p  has an empty value  p  is initialized on a separate execution path before the test for the empty value  For example, in the following function, my_test , the call impl()  assigns the value 4  to p . In the subsequent call, impl(n) , the assignment q = n  does not execute because p  is not empty. The statement y = q   reads q  before it is initialized, but the code generation software does not report an error. function y = my_test(n)impl();y = impl(n);end function y = impl(n)persistent p q; if nargin == 1    if isempty(p)        p = 2;        q = n;    end    y = q;else    p = 4;    endend ?
Workaround: Assign all persistent variables on all execution paths. For example, rewrite the function impl so that q is assigned a value when impl is called with no arguments:function y = impl(n)persistent p q; if nargin == 1    if isempty(p)        p = 2;        q = n;    end    y = q;else    p = 4;    q = 2;endend

Summary: Error Cannot instantiate Polyspace cluster during compilation phase
Description: On launching a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis, the following error occurs: Error: Cannot instantiate Polyspace cluster| Check the -scheduler option validity or your default cluster profile| Unable to checkout a license for the Parallel Computing Toolbox.  On the Configuration pane, under Distributed Computing, the "Batch " option is selected and grayed out.  This error occurs when you open a configuration from pre-R2013b Polyspace in a new version.
Workaround: Close Polyspace Bug Finder and save your configuration with a new name.Edit the new configuration file with an ASCII editor and remove the following line: <option flagname="-batch">true</option>

Summary: Using GetSet custom storage classes may degrade performance?
Description: Starting in release R2012a,  generated code efficiency for Simulink models using custom storage classes changes.  GetSet  custom storage class performance is slower than other custom storage classes. The attached model illustrates this problem. This model uses a For Iterator subsystem to update the elements of the Data Store Memory block. If the Data Store Memory block uses an ExportToFile  storage class, the Data Store Memory block is updated in place. No extra storage is allocated for the assignment block. If the Data Store Memory block uses a GetSet  custom storage class, the code generator allocates extra storage and generates an extra data copy.
Workaround: Download the attached optimizeCSC.zip file and unzip the file. Each time before you generate the code, execute the pcode file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initial value is not assigned to a property of a MATLAB? class in the generated code?
Description: A property of an object can have an incorrect value when all of the following conditions are true: The object is assigned to a persistent variable. The property has an initial value in the class definition, but the class constructor does not explicitly assign an initial value. The persistent variable is assigned in one call to the function where it is declared, but read in a separate call to that function.
Workaround: Identify the property whose value is incorrect.Initialize the property explicitly in the class constructor.

Summary: Code inspection fails when the generated code uses a direct pointer
Description: Code inspection fails when the generated code uses a direct pointer. For example, if your top model contains a referenced model, the top model start  function calls the referenced model start  function. If the calling argument is passed by reference, Embedded Coder can generate a direct pointer and pass it to the referenced model. Inspection of the generated code fails.

Summary: Simulink warns when closing or copying some models containing Scope blocks
Description: When you close one of the following Simulink models, the "Warning: Error evaluating 'DestroyFcn' callback of Scope block on model close" warning occurs.  If you copy the Scope block in these models this error occurs: "Error evaluating 'CopyFcn' callback of Scope block".The affected models are:dxpcImMultiHost in toolbox/rtw/targets/xpc/xpcdemos gm_fil_codegen_mimo_fil in toolbox/shared/eda/fil/fildemos stm32f4discovery_external_mode in toolbox/target/supportpackages/stm32f4discovery/stm32f4discoveryexamples
Workaround: Open the model in MATLAB, then run the attached cleancb.m script to remove the callbacks. This will dirty the model, but the model can then be saved or operated on without errors.Alternatively, you can open the "Properties" dialog box of each scope (by right clicking on the block to get the context menu), open the "Callbacks" tab, and interactively delete all callbacks marked with asterisks (*).

Summary: Code generation may generate an unused shared constant parameter
Description: The code generator creates an unused shared constant parameter variable in the shared utility directory under the following conditions:  The model contains a zero-value Constant block or a Ground block, where the block type is imported bus type. The model is part of a model reference hierarchy. The setting Code Generation > Interface > Shared  code placement  is set to Shared Location .  The code generator creates the unused shared constant parameter to hold the imported bus type ground value. The parameter is unused because the code generator creates and uses another constant variable in the model.c  or model.cpp  file to hold the ground value.   Note, the code is still correct, and produces the correct result.  The unused shared constant uses additional ROM.
Workaround: To work around the issue, set Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement to Auto.

Summary: Pressure and Temperature parameter checks in Thermal Liquid Settings block  are inaccurate
Description: Before simulation, the Thermal Liquid Settings block checks that the Pressure and Temperature vectors are in ascending order. These checks are inaccurate. They can trigger a false simulation error when a vector is in ascending order and fail to detect when a vector is not in ascending order. A false error is accompanied by a message of this type:Inconsistent mask parameters. Vector of Temperature must be arranged in strictly ascending order.
Workaround: To correct this issue, replace the thermal_liquid_settings.ssc file in your MATLAB installation with the attached version. The following instructions show you how to do this.Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.----------------Change log:April 9, 2014: attachment_1046776_13b_14a_2014-04-09.zip: fixed the check on temperature and pressure to ensure these vectors are in ascending order.

Summary: Enumerated data for 16-bit targets results in inefficient generated code
Description: When enumerated data is used in models or Stateflow charts, and code is generated for 16-bit targets, the code generator may generate inefficient code for: switch  statements Equality comparisons  The code generator may generate extra casts to the C long  type (32-bits). For switch  statements, the code generator may define extra temporary variables. For example, suppose that the code generator generates the following switch  statement where the case  values are enumerated type constants: void TestFunction(void){switch((Flavor)Test_Switch_U.e_flavor) {case Chocolate:case MapleWalnut:    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_darkbrown;    break;case Vanilla:    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_cream;    break;default:    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_unknown;    break;}} The code generator might now produce the following correct but less efficient code, adding the temporary variable guard1 , and theif  statement. void TestFunction(void){ boolean_T guard1 = FALSE; switch((Flavor)Test_Switch_U.e_flavor) {case Chocolate:    guard1 = TRUE;    break;case MapleWalnut:    guard1 = TRUE;    break;case Vanilla:    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_cream;break;default:    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_unknown;break;}if (guard1 == TRUE) {    Test_Switch_B.val = Color_darkbrown;}} ?

Summary: Copying and pasting multiple blocks in Simulink can hang MATLAB
Description: In the Simulink Editor, if you select multiple blocks and copy those blocks, when you paste those blocks, MATLAB may sometimes hang.
Workaround: If you encounter this problem, restart MATLAB and copy and paste the blocks one-by-one.

Summary: intlinprog throws an error when it exceeds the MaxTime time limit and there are no integer constraints
Description: intlinprog  throws an error when: There are no integer constraints (intcon  = [] ) The solution time exceeds the MaxTime  time limit option
Workaround: Increase the MaxTime option so that intlinprog returns before it exceeds the time limit.

Summary: Building a model produces repeated linker and compiler flags in the generated makefile.
Description: This bug affects models that satisfy these conditions: The Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > System target file  parameter is set to "ert.tlc". The Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Target Hardware  parameter is set to a value other than "None". For example, STMicroelectronics STM32F4-Discovery.  Building such a model two or more times successively results in the generated makefile containing repeated linker and compiler flags. If the model contains a Model block, even building the model once would result in repeated linker and compiler flags in the generated makefile. As a result of this bug, the executable generated by the build may not work as expected.  For example, with the STMicroelectronics STM32F4-Discovery board, the executable generated does not run on the hardware, and the following warnings are generated during the build: warning: redeclaration of memory region `FLASH' ...warning: redeclaration of memory region `SRAM'  Reproduction Steps  Download and install one of the following support packages using the supportPackageInstaller  command: ARM Cortex-A ARM Cortex-M STMicroelectronics STM32F4-Discovery Texas Instruments C2000 Xilinx Zynq-7000   Open any demo model. Make sure that the System target file is ert.tlc, and the Target hardware that is selected corresponds to the support package that was installed in step 1. Build the model, following the instructions in the documentation for that support package. Without changing anything in the model, rebuild the model.  The model should build successfully, but if you navigate to the code generation directory and look at the generated make file, you will find repeated Link Flags and Compiler Flags.

Summary: Code generation failure for complex function with inputs derived from dynamically sized matrices?
Description: When generating C code from MATLAB?  code that contains calls to the complex  function, if the inputs to complex  are derived from dynamically sized matrices, MATLAB can crash with a system error. The error message is similar to this message: Assertion at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\util_cg\sanitycheckerimpl.cpp line 1696: ValidCore violated: Types not equal: realPart->type(), imagPart->type() This error can occur when the code generation software cannot determine that the inputs to complex  have the same shape. For example, consider the following function: function y = complexify(ix)xre = ix(1:2:end-1);xim = ix(2:2:end);y = complex(xre, xim); If you generate code using the following command, code generation fails. codegen -args {coder.typeof(0, [1 12], [false true])} -config:mex complexify ?

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for a model containing a Width block

The Width block has an output signal with a storage class other than Auto .

Summary: dicomread scales values for particular HighBit metadata values
Description: DICOM files with bit-depths that don't span 8- or 16-bits might be read incorrectly by dicomread , causing values to be scaled by a power of two. For example, consider a DICOM file with the following metadata: BitsAllocated = 16 BitsStored = 12 HighBit = 15  The pixels are stored in a 16-bit "pixel cell," where the actual pixel data is in the upper 12 bits of the 16-bit cell. Pixel values that should be in the range [32 4080] are being read in the range [512 65280] because dicomread  is not masking out the lower 4 bits appropriately.

Summary: Error when simulating a model with linked library Stateflow charts when Fixed-point instrumentation mode is set to Minimums, maximums and overflows
Description: Simulink throws an assertion error when you simulate a model that contains a linked library Stateflow chart, State Transition Table, or Truth Table, and the Fixed-point instrumentation mode  is set to Minimums, maximums and overflows . You can control this parameter using either the Fixed-Point Tool or set_param('MySys','MinMaxOverflowLogging','MinMaxAndOverflow')  on any parent subsystem or the model itself. The generated stack trace begins with the following error message: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\cdr\cdr_types.cpp at line 546:                 fSimStructToHandleMap->size() == 1

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checks fails to detect when configuration parameter GenerateAllocFcn is set to on???
Description: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility check Check code generation settings  fails to detect when configuration parameter Generate function to allocate model data  is set to on . To be compatible with code inspection, Generate function to allocate model data  must be set to off . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Generate function to allocate model data  is on the Code Generation > Interface  pane. The command-line form of the parameter is GenerateAllocFcn . Generate function to allocate model data  only appears for ERT-based targets with Code interface packaging  set to Reusable function . ?
Workaround: 1. In the Configuration Parmaters dialog box, on the Code Generation > Interface pane, clear Generate function to allocate model data.2. Regenerate code.3. Proceed with inspection.

Summary: Using a gpuArray indexing expression containing a colon (:) as a counter in a loop causes MATLAB to crash.
Description: Using a gpuArray indexing expression containing a colon (:) as a counter in a loop causes MATLAB to crash. For example, the following script test.m  uses a gpuArray object C  in a loop expression: % test.m  A = gpuArray(rand(3));  B = gpuArray(rand(3));  C = gpuArray(2);  for i = 1:C  A = B(1,1:i);  end  Doing the folloiwng from MATLAB command line will cause MATLAB to crash >> test>> clear all>> test  ?
Workaround: Avoid using gpuArray object in a loop expression.

Summary: A model that uses an implicit ODE solver fails to build if slrt.tlc or slrtert.tlc is set as the system target file.
Description: A model that requires an implicit ODE solver (ode14x) generates a build error if that model is set to use the slrt.tlc or slrtert.tlc system target file. These models often contain physmod blocks using continuous states. If a model was using xpctarget.tlc in a previous release of MATLAB, when that model is loaded in R2014a, it is switched automatically to slrt.tlc as part of our product renaming from xPC Target to Simulink Real-Time.
Workaround: A model can be manually switched back to use xpctarget.tlc. There are two ways to do it. On the Code Generation tab of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, you can type in the name xpctarget.tlc or xpctargetert.tlc. (These choices are no longer on the pull down menu, but you can type them in.) From the MATLAB command prompt, open the model and use the following command set_param( gcs, 'SystemTargetFile', 'xpctarget.tlc' ); The automatic switch on model load from target file xpctarget.tlc to slrt.tlc can be suppressed by creating a file with the name keep_xpctarget.dat in the slrtprefdir, as follows:fclose(fopen(fullfile(slrtprefdir, 'keep_xpctarget.dat'), 'a'));

Summary: Block linked to library-based masked Sum block changes in size
Description: Consider a model that contains a linked block that is associated with a masked Sum block stored in a library. When you save this model then reopen it, Simulink changes the size of the linked block. When displaying the changed block size, Simulink overrides the block size that is saved in the model.

Summary: Builder NE cannot create strongly named .NET Assemblies
Description: In Builder NE, the "Create Shared Assembly" option does not work in R2013b and R2014a because the Library Compiler App generates an invalid command line for mcc . The comma between the framework version and the key file is missing. The resulting assembly is thus not signed.
Workaround: As a workaround, use the MATLAB mcc command to generate a .NET Assembly instead of using deploytool.mcc -W dotnet:component_name,className,framework_version,security,remote_type -T link:lib mfilename...Another way to obtain the appropriate mcc command is from the package log generated by deploytool. Package the .NET Assembly normally. When packaging completes, look at the generated log file PackagingLog.txt. Copy the mcc command generated by deploytool. The command is located near the top of the file, and looks like:mcc -W 'dotnet:Title,Class1,0.0C:\Users\...\keyPair.snk' -T link:lib -d c:\Users\...\Title\for_testing -v C:\Users\...\hello\function.m class{Class1:C:\Users\...\programs\function.m}Add the missing comma between the framework version and the keyfile location. Then run the mcc command from the MATLAB command window like:mcc -W 'dotnet:Title,Class1,0.0,C:\Users\...\keyPair.snk' -T link:lib -d c:\Users\...\Title\for_testing -v C:\Users\...\hello\function.m class{Class1:C:\Users\...\programs\function.m}Notice the added comma between '0.0' and 'C:\Users'.

Summary: Enumerated data types on 16-bit targets cause unexpected behavior
Description: When using an enum data type and generating code for a 16-bit target: An assertion can happen when a signal with bus type containing an enum data type element is used by Simulink and either a MATLAB Function block or Stateflow block. The code generator can create unnecessary casts, casting enum variables to a 32-bit long data type. The compiler uses 32-bit registers for comparisons instead of 16-bit registers, as in previous releases. The code executes correctly, but can be less efficient and less readable.

Summary: Deleting a supertransition might result in Stateflow model corruption that crashes MATLAB upon loading
Description: Deleting a supertransition with endpoints in two different subcharts, then converting one of the subcharts back to its original form, and saving the model might result in corruption. The resulting corrupted model crashes when loading.
Workaround:
If your model is corrupted, you can still recover nearly all of it by removing all super-transition data from the model file. Backup your model first.Instructions for fixing a *.mdl file:Open the model file in a text editorSearch for "transition" blocks: transition {...}Remove all "transition" blocks containing type SUPERor type SUBSave the model fileInstructions for fixing a *.slx file:Open the model file like a zip file (either associate .slx with .zip or simply rename the file)Inside the .slx file, open blockdiagram.xml in a text editorSearch for "transition" blocks: ...Remove all "transition" blocks containing:

SUPER

or

SUB

Save your edits (including updating the zip file)

Summary: Reading numbers from a file using textscan can return incorrect output
Description: The textscan  function can return incorrect output when reading a file. In particular, textscan  returns an incorrect file position when it encounters a number that immediately precedes a sign character without a trailing imaginary number that includes i  or j . Subsequent calls to textscan  might return incorrect data. For example, consider the string, '123-456' . str = '123-456'; When reading from a string, textscan  returns the correct position.[C, position] = textscan(str, '%f', 2)C =    [2x1 double]position =    7  However, when reading the same data from a file, textscan  returns an incorrect file position. fileID = fopen('foo.txt', 'w');fprintf(fileID, '%s', str);fclose(fileID);fileID = fopen('foo.txt', 'r');[C, position] = textscan(fileID, '%f', 2)C =    [2x1 double]position =    4fclose(fileID); Subsequent calls to textscan  on the same file may return incorrect data due to the incorrect file position.
Workaround: Read a file line by line and feed the output string to textscan without changing the other arguments.line = fgetl(fileID);[C, position] = textscan(line, ...);

Summary: Possible incorrect behavior of generated code for the typecast function when C compiler char type is not 8 bits
Description: Code generation for the typecast  function generates a call to the C run-time memcpy  function. The computation of the number of bytes to copy is based on an 8-bit C char type. On platforms where the char type is larger than 8 bits, it is possible that too many bytes are copied.
Workaround: You need the following information for your target platform: nbx ¡ª the number of bytes per element of the input array xnby ¡ª the number of bytes per element of the output class datatypeReplace the call to the typecast function with the following code: nbytes = numel(x)*nbx; % number of bytes to copyny = nbytes/nby; % length of the output array (must be an integer) y = cast(zeros(1,ny),datatype); % allocate the output arrayptr = coder.opaque('void*'); % define the output of memcpyptr = coder.ceval('memcpy',coder.wref(y(1)),coder.rref(x(1)),uint32(nbytes));

Summary: DAQ Session 'Release' method does not free hardware reserved by inputSingleScan or outputSingleScan operations.
Description: When using inputSingleScan or outputSingleScan, the hardware used by a session is reserved. calling Release does not properly unreserved this hardware.This is only an issue when using multiple sessions to access channels on the same subsystem of a device. Example:ao1 = daq.createSession('ni');ao1.addAnalogOutputChannel('dev1', 'ao0', 'Voltage');ao1.outputSingleScan([0]);ao1.release();ao2= daq.createSession('ni');ao2.addAnalogOutputChannel('dev1', 'ao0', 'Voltage');ao2.outputSingleScan([0]); The hardware associated with this session is reserved. If you are using it in another session use the release function to unreserve the hardware. If you are using it in an external program exit that program. Then try this operation again.

Caused by:Matrix dimensions must agree." "Error evaluating 'StopFcn' callback of Sigbuilder block block (mask) 'untitled/Signal Builder'. Caused by:Matrix dimensions must agree."
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Signals not marked as test-points in a referenced model using accelerator mode may not display in a scope viewer.
Description: The Signal Selector allows you to select signals inside a referenced model that are marked for logging, but are not marked as a test point. Such signals may not display in a scope viewer when the logging format for the model is set to Dataset and the Model block simulation mode is set to Accelerator. A warning message is issued to alert to a problem with logging, but it does not clearly help to resolve the situation.
Workaround: Mark the signal you want to view with a test point. 1. Righ-click a signal in a referenced model to display the contet menu, and then select Properties. 2. In the Signal Properties dialog box, select the Test point check box.Alternatively, set the referenced model signal logging format to ModelDataLog.1. From the referenced model menu, select Simulation > Model Configuration Parameters. 2. In the left pane, select the Data Import/Export tab. 3. From the Signal logging format drop down list, select ModelDataLog.Alternatively, set the referenced model simulation mode to Normal.1. From the referenced model menu, select Simulation > Model  > Normal.

Summary: FlexLM license is not released when a job is canceled/deleted
Description: On Windows, when a batch job is sent to an MDCS cluster, the license taken by the job is not released when this job is canceled/deleted.  This will lead to an error: "License checkout failed. License Manager Error -4 Maximum number of users for PolySpace_Server_C_CPP reached."
Workaround: There are two  patches available that must be installed on the Windows server(s).  Choose the patch that matches the version of MATLAB you have installed (32 or 64 bits).Installation instructions for R2014a Polyspace server1. Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.2. Locate your MATLAB installation for the version to patch. By default this is in C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2014a. This folder is referred as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. 3. Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.4. Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file, open the .zip file and look at the contents.5. Extract the files from the .zip file to your <MATLAB> folder6. Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.7. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Code inspection gives incorrect output under certain conditions if the model contains a Selector block feeding a Polynomial block
Description: Code inspection gives incorrect output if the model contains a Selector block feeding a Polynomial block and the first element selected by the Selector block is not the first element of the input signal. For example, the Index Option  is Index vector (dialog)  and the Index  is [2 3] , or Index Option  is Starting index (dialog)  and Index  is 2 . The inspection result will be reported as "Failed to Verify" even if the result should have been "Verified" or "Partially Verified".

Summary: Cloning the comm.MemorylessNonlinearity object does not copy some of the properties.
Description: The properties AMAMParameters and AMPMParameters are not copied when cloning an unlocked comm.MemorylessNonlinearity object if the "Ghorbani" or the "Saleh" model is used.
Workaround: A possible workaround would be to call the step method prior to cloning. The object will then copy all the properties.

Summary: Model Explorer loading of contents can be slow if "Show/Hide Masked Subsystems" and "Show/Hide Library Links" options are enabled
Description: In Model Explorer, if a model has many masked subsystems and library links, loading contents for that model takes long time if Show/Hide Masked Subsystem  and Show/Hide Library Links  are on. The situation becomes worse if Current System and Below  setting is also on.
Workaround: Turn off one or more of the following Model Explorer settings:Show/Hide Masked Subsystem Show/Hide Library LinksCurrent System and Below

Summary: Possible wrong answers from code generated for parfor-loops using Microsoft? Windows? compiler
Description: When the target compiler is Microsoft?  Visual C++?  2012 (Version 11.0) or earlier, the generated code for a parfo r -loop containing functions that take scalar complex or fixed-point arguments may produce wrong answers. ?
Workaround: In R2014a and beyond, upgrade to Microsoft? Visual C++? 2013 (Version 12.0) or later.In prior releases the Visual C++? 2013 compiler was not supported.  Alternative workarounds are to use for loops instead of  parfor loops; or to rewrite your algorithm to avoid passing scalar complex and scalar fixed-point values to functions inside the body of your parfor loop.

Summary: Divide by zero or floating-point exception during code generation due to subscripting into an empty array
Description: When the MATLAB?   code contains an indexing expression with multiple subscripts indexing into an empty matrix, the code generation software can produce a stack trace with a "divide by zero" or "floating point exception" error message. This error can occur for indexing expressions on the left or right side of an assignment. In this example, the indexing expression on the right side of an assignment causes the error. function y = foo()  x = zeros(0,2);  y = x(:,end);end In this example, the indexing expression on the left side of an assignment causes the error. function y = foo(x)  y = [];  y(:, 1) = x;end In this example, selecting a subset of the columns with the indexing expression on the right side of an assignment causes the error. function y = foo(n)  y = [];  y = y(:,1:n);end In this example, growing an array with the indexing expression on the left side of an assignment causes the error. Note that growing an array by indexing is not supported by code generation and causes an  error at run time. function y = foo(n)  y = [];  for k = 1:n    y(:,end+1) = k;  endend This error can occur when the following MATLAB functions are called with empty arguments: orth , null , deblank , strtrim   This error can occur when typecast  is called with an empty string for the data type. Note that calling typecast  with an empty string argument is an error in MATLAB.
Workaround: Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems*:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  *Notes for Windows Systems:      You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.  To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.    If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you  may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to  WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting  Run as administrator.    From  within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.    Extract  the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Error "ps_porky.exe has stopped working" during compilation phase
Description: When you run a Polyspace Code Prover verification on C++ code, the analysis may fail during the compilation phase with the following pop-up error message: "ps_porky.exe has stopped working". This type of error is specific to the code being verified. So if you see this error, contact Technical Support. If possible, please provide the source code and the verification log.

Summary: Removing a running job from Polyspace Queue Manager freezes Job Scheduler
Description: This error occurs if you perform the following steps:  Using Polyspace Bug Finder (or polyspace-bug-finder.exe command), run an analysis using 'Batch' mode.  Then launch a second analysis in 'Batch' mode.  Open the Polyspace Queue Manager  Remove the first analysis job (while it is running) from queue Then, check the status of the next job.  You can see that it remains in a queued status.  Note that there is no refresh effect, jobs are still in a queued state.

Summary: Code inspection gives incorrect output under certain conditions if the model contains a MinMax block
Description: Code inspection gives incorrect output if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a MinMax block The input of the MinMax block is a single vector input The configuration option Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Optimize global data access  is set to something other than None   The inspection result will be reported as "Failed to Verify" even if the result should have been "Verified" or "Partially Verified".
Workaround: To work around this issue, consider changing your model or its configuration such that one of the above conditions is no longer true; for example, set the configuration option Optimization > Signals and Parameters > Optimize global data access to None.

Summary: ActiveX? controls are not visible in a GUIDE GUI figure
Description: When you launch a previously saved GUIDE GUI, Microsoft? ActiveX controls in the GUI figure are not visible.

Summary: Dragging blocks from the Library Browser into a Simulink model can crash Simulink
Description: When you drag a block from the Library Browser into a Simulink model, very rarely this can cause Simulink to crash.

Summary: Error message incorrectly claims that output variables are not assigned on all paths inside graphical functions on a MATLAB action language chart.
Description: In some cases, Stateflow incorrectly complains that an output variable is not assigned on all paths inside a graphical function. This happens only on MATLAB action language charts despite the variable actually being assigned on all paths.
Workaround: At the start of the flow graph, before any flow graph branching, initialize all variables that the error message claims are not assigned to on all pathways.

Summary: imfilter is slow when filtering multi-channel or 3D images with separable 2D kernels
Description: imfilter  can be used to filter a 3D image or a multi-channel image using a 2D kernel. imfilter  applies the 2D kernel filtering internally to the input, a plane at a time. If the 2D kernel is separable, then the performance of imfilter  is slower than expected.
Workaround: imfilter performance with using a 2D seperable kernel on a 3D or a multi-channel image can be improved by manually processing the image one plane at a time.For example, this simple call to imfilter:im = rand(100,100,3);h = ones(6);o = imfilter(im,h)?can be replaced with a faster code fragment:o = zeros(size(im),'like',im);for zInd = 1:size(im,3)o(:,:,zInd) = imfilter(im(:,:,k),h);endIf additional input arguments were used in the first call, ensure to retain them for the second.??

Summary: copyfile fails on RHEL5
Description: The copyfile  function attempts to preserve mode, ownership and timestamp when copying files. However, on Red Hat? Enterprise Linux? 5, the preservation of ownership causes copyfile  to return an error.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when changing the Color property for multiple lines at once through the Property Editor
Description: Changing the Color  property of multiple line objects at once via the Property Editor  may cause MATLAB to crash. This behavior occurs only when the lines have different colors originally.
Workaround: A workaround to this behavior can be accomplished by avoiding to change the Color property of multiple line objects within the Property Editor at once. Instead, use the Property Editor to select and change the Color property of individual line objects one at a time.To change the Color property of multiple line objects at once, you can use the set command. For example, the following command will change the color of lines h1 and h2 to red.set([h1 h2],'Color','Red');
